item.title: 我们在 Github 上流行，我们就是这样做的🤯 | We are trending on Github, This is how we did it 🤯
item.description:
老实说，Github 有一个非凡的算法，几乎不可能知道那里发生了什么。
我们确实有一点感觉，今天想和大家分享一下！

在我们开始之前，如果你能帮我主演 ⭐️ 我们的图书馆 Novu，我会很激动！ 所以我们可以继续保持趋势。

我们知道，要成为趋势，您必须有很多精确的点可以引导您的图书馆进行一些活动。 可以是 Github star、Issue、Pull request 等。

一切都归功于这个出色的 DEV 社区和开源社区！

文章

上周我们从 dev.to 上的一篇文章开始，该文章获得了超级热门！
这篇文章变得如此流行，以至于我们登上了daily.dev，在许多其他网站上转发/发布。 然后该系列的后续也获得了很多流量

那太棒了！ 我们还发布了关于开源的文章：

如何为开源公司筹集资金💵

只是分享一点细节：

推特

我们收到了 Vercel 创始人 Guillermo Rauch 的推文。 完全出乎意料🙏🏻。 并获得了更多推文和转发。


红迪网

我们在 Reddit 上发布了我们正在流行的消息，获得了更多的明星，并从 Typescript 的流行趋势转向了全球趋势。

综上所述

我们没有计划，但我们非常兴奋它发生在我们身上。 我们希望它会持续很长时间。 这真的有助于 Novu 和社区的成长 ⭐️ 如果您能帮助我们获得一颗星星，那就太棒了！

一旦暂停，novu 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，novu 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有 novu 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 novu 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 novu 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 novu 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

To be honest, Github has an extraordinary algorithm, and it's pretty impossible to know what going on back there.
We do have a slight feeling I want to share with you today!

Before we start, I would be thrilled if you could help me by starring ⭐️ our library Novu! So we can keep on being Trending.

https://github.com/novuhq/novu

We know that to be trending, you must have a lot of pinpoints that lead to your library with some activity. That can be a Github star, Issue, Pull request, etc.

Everything is thanks to this fantastic DEV community and the open-source community!

Articles

We started last week with an article on dev.to that got super Trending!
How to build a real-time Auction System with Socket.io and React.js 🤯

This article became so trending that we got on daily.dev, retweeted/posted on many other websites. And then the follow-up of the series got a lot of traffic also

How to build a real-time Auction system - Connecting Socket.io With React 🔥

So that was awesome! We also released our article about open-source:

How to raise funds for an open-source company 💵

Just to share a bit of detail:

Twitter

We got tweeted by Vercel founder Guillermo Rauch. Totally unexpected 🙏🏻. And got many more tweets and retweets.


Reddit

We posted on Reddit that we are trending, got many more stars, and moved from trending on Typescript to global Trending.

In conclusion

We didn't plan that, but we are super excited that it happened to us. We hope it will last for a long time. It really helps Novu and the community to grow ⭐️ If you can help us with a star, that would be amazing!

https://github.com/novuhq/novu

item.title: 通用无密码身份验证 - 开始 | Universal passwordless authentication - The beginning
item.description:
很长一段时间以来，我一直在玩弄制作身份验证平台的想法......我开始这样做了！ 我目前正在休育儿假，从公司生活中解脱出来，在我晚上的时间，当孩子们熟睡时，我想我会试一试。

我总是发现身份验证环境有点坏。 对于用户和开发人员。 一方面，对于用户而言，您必须在每个该死的网站上单独注册，密码复杂得令人沮丧，而您的祖母无论如何又是网络钓鱼攻击的受害者。 另一方面，仅仅实现一个安全的身份验证系统是一项耗时的工作......并且有很多意想不到的复杂性（是的，我看着你 OAuth2！）。 如果你想用 MFA 做一些真正安全的事情，天哪……好吧，难怪有这么多付费身份提供者。 只是为了什么？ 只是为了验证用户。

就这样吧，让我们为每个人简化一下！ 具有无密码身份验证的全局身份。 只需将手指放在手机传感器上或查看笔记本电脑的摄像头即可注册/登录，仅此而已。 如果你想尝试一下，这里是：

为所有人使用 TouchID 或 FaceID 进行身份验证。 更少的密码，更高的安全性！

这一切都归功于最近一个名为 webauthn 的 w3c 协议。 然而，无论这个协议多么出色，它也有一个很大的阴暗面：它的复杂性。 数百页的规范、晦涩的二进制数据结构、不直观的极端案例等等。

好吧，这就是为什么做一个“开箱即用的服务”对我来说是有意义的，这就是我试图在这里建立的。 理想情况下，每个人都是免费的，由一些神奇的赞助支持，但现在对我来说这听起来像是乌托邦。 目前，它还处于起步阶段。 这就像一个婴儿只是在四处张望，等待在优质的服务中长大。 哎呀，它甚至还没有合适的域名！

那么为什么我要展示这么早，远未完成，完全混乱的东西呢？ 好吧，收集兴趣。 作为一个单独的开发者，在没有任何反馈的情况下在虚空中进行开发是一种孤独……而且你的脑海中总是萦绕着诸如“值得吗？”之类的问题。 和“有人会使用它吗？”。 所以，如果你喜欢你目前看到的，请为我加油！ 感谢您的阅读！

克里斯蒂安·埃斯塔利奇—— 一旦暂停，dagnelies 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，dagnelies 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有 dagnelies 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 dagnelies 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Since quite some time now, I've toyed around the idea of making an authentication platform ...and I'm starting doing it! I'm currently on a parental leave, freed from the corporate life and in my evening hours, when the kids are sound asleep, I thought I would give this a shot.

I always found the authentication landscape kind of broken. For both users and developers. On one side, for users, you have to register separately on every damn website, with frustratingly complex passwords while your grandma is again the victim of a phishing attack anyway. On the other side of the fence, just implementing a secure authentication system a time consuming piece of work ...and with a lot of unexpected complexity (yeah, I look at you OAuth2!). And if you want something really safe with MFA, oh boy ...well, no wonder there are so many paid identity providers. Just for what? Just to authenticate users.

So here it goes, let's simplify that for everyone! Global identities with passwordless authentication. Just put your finger on your phone sensor or look at your laptop's camera to register/login, that's it. If you want to try it out, here it is:

Authentication using TouchID or FaceID for everyone. Less passwords, more security!

This is all thanks to a recent w3c protocol called webauthn. However, no matter how great this protocol is, it also has a big shadow side: its sheer complexity. Hundreds of pages of specs, obscure binary data structures, unintuitive corner cases and so on.

So well, that's why making a "working out of the box service" would make sense to me, and that is what I'm attempting to build here. Ideally for free for everyone, sustained by some magical sponsorships, but this sounds like utopia to me right now. Currently, it's, ahem, just at its early infancy. It's like a baby just looking around, waiting to grow up in a fine service. Heck, it does not even have a proper domain name yet!

So why am I showing such an early, far from finished, completely messy thing? Well, to gather interest. As a solo dev, it's kind of lonely to just develop in the void without any feedback ...and your head is haunted with questions like "is it worth it?" and "will anybody use it anyway?". So, if you like what you see so far, please cheer for me! :D You are very welcome to also tell me what you would like to see next, or just tell me you liked it so far. Thank you for reading!

Cristian Estarlich - Aug 10

item.title: Appwrite 社区报告 #18 | Appwrite Community Report #18
item.description:
用于Appwrite的haimantika mitra

发表于大家好！ 我们带着每周一次的社区报告回来了，与大家分享 Appwrite 一直在做的事情！ 向您展示来自 Appwrite 团队的一些新发布的视频、已解决的问题、正在进行的工作以及更多与 Appwrite 相关的内容。 请继续关注到最后！ 我们刚刚宣布了 OSS 基金的第三个项目

如果您是维护者或认识希望获得支持的维护者，请告诉他们有关Appwrite OSS 基金的信息

这是为了让 Appwrite 每天都变得更好！ 本周我们处理的问题：

  • 从响应模型中删除了常量
    修复了代码显示，无法正确呈现某些输入
    修复了 Appwrite 中成功密码重置的警报和重定向
    重构使用统计数据以从已收集的较低时间段统计数据中聚合更多统计数据，而不是使用流入数据库
    重构权限级别

  • 重构 Appwrite 的查询
  • 在运行时添加控制台日志捕获功能
  • 社区成员Garvitomer添加了 fast2sms 电话提供商

说说 Appwrite 的查询
与 Appwrite 升级保持同步的教程

    haimantika mitra for Appwrite

    Posted on Aug 12

    Howdy people! 👋

    We are back with our weekly community report, to share with you all what Appwrite has been doing! 🤩

    TLDR; Presenting before you some newly released videos from the Appwrite team, issues solved, work-in-progress and more Appwrite related stuff. Stay tuned till the end! 😉

    📢 What’s new

    • We just announced the third project for the OSS Fund
      📢 Announcing the fourth sponsored project of the Appwrite OSS Fund - Lazygit!

      OSS Fund is an initiative by Appwrite to support maintainers like @DuffieldJesse 🎉

      🔗 Applications are still open, get funded or spread the word - appwrite.io/oss-fund

    If you are a maintainer or know a maintainer who would love some support, tell them about the Appwrite OSS Fund

    ✅ Issues solved

    Here’s to making Appwrite better everyday! Issues we have worked on this week:

    • Removed constants from response models
      🔗Link to PR

    • Fixed code displays, not correctly rendering certain input
      🔗Link to PR

    • Fixed alert and redirect of successful password resets in Appwrite
      🔗Link to PR

    • Refactored usage stats to aggregate more stats from already collected lower time period stats, instead of using influx db
      🔗Link to PR

    ⚒️ What we’re currently working on

    • Refactoring permission levels
    • Refactoring Appwrite’s queries
    • Addition of console log capturing ability in runtimes
    • Addition of fast2sms phone provider by community member Garvitomer

    🗣 Discussions

    Let’s talk about Appwrite’s queries
    🔗Join the discussion here

    📘 Resources

    • Tutorial on Staying Up to Date With Appwrite Upgrades
    • Building an Open Source Search App With Appwrite and Meilisearch
    • Learning Appwrite in 30 days

      30 Days of Appwrite is a month long event focused at teaching you about all of Appwrite's core concepts and getting you ready to build production ready apps with Appwrite.

    Suraj Vishwakarma - Aug 12

Happy Friday, friends! 🎉 Follow Friday is your weekly opportunity to shout out fellow DEV Community members doing awesome work. Check out the comments and follow someone new!

Go (also known as Golang) is an open source programming language that was designed at Google in 2009. It's a fast, statically typed, compiled language that feels like a dynamically typed, interpreted language — and its users love it for its expressive, concise, clean, and efficient syntax.

Plus, the language boasts one of the best mascots out there (sorry to Ferris the Crab).

So, devs: who are your favorite DEV community members writing about Go?
