item.title: 每个 DBA 都需要了解透明数据加密 (TDE) 吗？ | Does every DBA need to understand Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)?
item.description:
他们有责任根据公司的标准化和良好的开发实践定期分析银行以确保系统没有过载，并检查服务器的目的地是否正确。 除了这些功能，DBA 还可以：

  • 以完美的完整性和可用性交付数据
  • 与安全和开发团队合作
  • 确保数据库查询的最大性能

密码学是一种可用于维护数据库信息的机密性和保护的技术。 目标是使用加密来防止未经授权的人访问存储的数据。 只有拥有适当加密密钥的人才能查看它，从而保护数据免受恶意个人甚至恶意软件的侵害。

静态数据

在深入研究 TDE（透明数据加密）的工作原理之前，我们需要了解静态数据的概念。 简而言之，我们可以说静止数据是指数据已到达目的地并且未被访问或使用时。 该数据可以包括分类为结构化和非结构化数据的数据。 此类数据可以是很少或从不更改的存档文件，没有任何类型的恒常性。

在信息安全的背景下，这些类型的数据很容易被黑客威胁访问，从而以数字或物理方式获得访问权限。 为了保证其安全，公司选择通过加密或与密钥保护相结合的方式采用安全保护措施。

与移动中的数据和使用中的数据相比，此类数据相对更容易保护，但重要的是要谨慎选择最佳预防措施，因为它不会经常使用，还必须实施良好实践。

我们可以得出结论，静态数据是已写入磁盘的数据，因为当写入磁盘时，它会产生保护间隙：

  • 我们数据库的任何数据文件
  • 我们数据库的任何日志文件
  • 数据库的所有备份文件，无论是完整备份、日志备份还是差异备份
  • 数据库快照文件
  • 在 TempDB 数据库中写入磁盘的任何数据

透明数据加密 (TDE)

这种类型的加密适用于 SQL Server、Azure 数据库和 Azure SQL 数据仓库数据文件。

开发的基础是整个加密过程对访问和使用数据库的应用程序完全透明。 如何？ 这很奇怪：使用高级加密标准 (AES)或三重 DES ，文件页面被加密，然后在信息到达内存时被解密。 它结束了从加密数据库查询数据的任何限制。 一个超级有趣的优势是，即使使用复杂的处理，数据库的大小也不会增加，而是保持不变。

使用 TDE 时，数据库的备份也被加密，这意味着如果该备份发生问题，只能使用其设置期间使用的证书、密码和密钥进行恢复。

在 TDE 配置期间，您可以指定要用于加密的AES 算法：AES_128、AES_192 或 AES_256 - 数字指定将用于加密的密钥的长度（以位为单位）。 密钥越大，解密就越困难。 例如，在选择 AES_128 时，我们谈论的是一千多年。 但在选择的那一刻，唯一关心的应该是未来几年该技术可能会发展多少。 那么，剩下的问题是：X年后，解密还需要一千年吗？

一个非常有趣的功能是自动对 TempDB 进行加密。 毕竟，所有用户数据库都使用 tempdb 来处理和存储临时对象。 尽管不完全可见，但此过程在开发过程中展示了强大的安全性和专业性。

执行

为了演示该过程的简单性，我们可以按以下顺序理解其操作：

  1. 创建主密钥
  2. 创建受主密钥保护的证书
  3. 创建数据库加密密钥
  4. 激活加密
  5. 备份证书

如果您是 DBA，您很有可能负责保护敏感和/或机密信息。 这也意味着确保了解最新和最先进的隐私增强技术，以确保数据、企业和个人的安全。 我们收集了几篇文章，可以帮助您完成这项任务：

在 Vaultree，我们正在构建一个加密的未来。 我们喜欢分享有价值的信息和趋势，以帮助您保护数据安全。 注册以了解最新动态，并与专家团队讨论网络安全领域最热门的趋势。



原文:

A DBA (Database Administrator) is the professional responsible for the creation, installation, monitoring, repair and analysis of the database architecture. They have the responsibility of analysing the banks periodically so that there are no overloads in the system, and also to check that the destination of the servers is correct, according to the company's standardisation and good development practices. In addition to these functions, a DBA can also:

  • Deliver data in perfect integrity and availability
  • Work with the security and development teams
  • Ensure maximum performance for database queries

Cryptography is a technique that can be used to maintain the secrecy and protection of database information. The goal is to use encryption to prevent unauthorised people from gaining access to stored data. Only those with the appropriate encryption key can view it, keeping data safe from malicious individuals, and even malware.

Data at rest

Before we delve into how TDE (Transparent Data Encryption) works, we need to understand the concept of Data at Rest. In short we can say that a data at rest is when data has arrived at a destination and is not being accessed or used. This data can include data classified as structured and unstructured data. Such data can be archive files that are rarely or never changed, without any kind of constancy.

In the context of information security, these types of data are easily accessible to hacker threats to gain access digitally or physically. In order to keep it secure, companies choose to employ security protection measures through encryption, or a combination with key protection.

This type of data is relatively easier to protect compared to data in motion and data in use, but it's important to choose the best preventative measure with care, as it is not in constant use and good practices must also be implemented.

We can conclude that data at rest, is data that has been written to disk, because when written to disk, it creates a protection gap:

  • Any data files for our database
  • Any log files for our database
  • All backup files for the database, be they Full, Log or Differential backups
  • Database snapshot files
  • Any data written to disk in the TempDB database

Transparent Data Encryption (TDE)

The main purpose of TDE is to protect data by encrypting physical files, i.e. data "at rest". This type of encryption works on SQL Server, Azure Database and Azure SQL Data Warehouse data files.

The development was based so that the whole encryption process was completely transparent to the applications that access and use the database. How? It's quite curious: Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) or Triple DES is used, the file pages are encrypted and then decrypted as the information reaches memory. It puts an end to any limitations on querying data from an encrypted database. One super interesting advantage is that even using complex processing, the size of the database does not increase, it remains constant.

TDE works by using an encryption key that is stored in the database being encrypted - but this key is stored encrypted by an object outside the database. When using TDE the backups of the database are also encrypted, meaning that if something happens to that backup, it can only be restored with the certificate, passwords and keys used during its setup.

During TDE configuration you can specify which AES algorithm you wish to use for encryption: AES_128, AES_192 or AES_256 - The number specifies the length of the key that will be used for encryption in bits. The bigger the key the harder it will be to decrypt. When choosing AES_128, for example, we're talking about more than a thousand years. But at the moment of choosing, the only concern should be how much the technology might evolve in the next few years. So, the remaining question is: X years from now, will it still take a thousand years to decrypt?

TempDB is encrypted too!

"The tempdb system database is a global resource that holds: Temporary user objects that are explicitly created. They include global or local temporary tables and indexes, temporary stored procedures, table variables, tables returned in table-valued functions, and cursors" - Microsoft Docs

A very interesting feature is that automatically TempDB will be encrypted. After all, all user databases use tempdb to process and store temporary objects. As much as it is not fully viewable, this process demonstrates a great power of security and professionalism during development.

Implementation

The implementation is easily cited in documentation from Oracle and SQL Server. To demonstrate the simplicity of the process we can understand its operation in the following order:

  1. Creation of the master key
  2. Create certificate protected by master key
  3. Create database encryption key
  4. Activation of encryption
  5. Backing up certificate

DBA Responsibilities

If you are a DBA, there is a good chance that you are in charge of protecting sensitive and/or confidential information. That means also making sure to be aware of the latest and most advanced privacy enhancing technologies to keep data, businesses and individuals safe. We gathered a few articles that might help you on this mission:

At Vaultree we are building an encrypted future. We love sharing valuable information and trends to help you keep your data safe. Sign up to stay in the loop and discuss the hottest trends in cybersec with a team of experts.
item.author: lorendev

item.title: 我如何使用 Python 自动更新我的 Twitter 个人资料。 | How I update my Twitter profile automatically using Python.
item.description:
维尼修斯·康司·恩纳里

发表于我发现自动化任务是编程中最酷的事情之一。 最近，我一直在思考如何利用 API 来自动化一些事情，我想出了让我的 Twitter 个人资料横幅图像不时自动更改的想法。 这就像访问没有人拥有的 Twitter 功能，将幻灯片作为横幅而不是单个图像的能力。

我将使用NASA 的每日天文图片API 来获取我将使用的图像。 此 API 每天提供不同的图像，使其适合此特定目的。 此外，通过使用此 API，我知道我的 Twitter 横幅不会重复任何图片。 您可以使用来自其他 API 或您自己的图像。

获取对 API 的访问权限

在开始之前，您需要访问 Twitter API 和 NASA API。

推特 API

要访问 Twitter API，您必须访问Twitter 的开发者门户。 确保使用您希望横幅自动更改的相同帐户登录门户。 我不会逐步介绍获取 Twitter API 访问权限所需执行的操作，但这里是指南。 您需要申请对 API 的提升访问权限（第四步），因为更新个人资料的请求是 v1.1 端点的一部分。 获得更高的访问权限并不难。 您必须解释您将如何使用 API，以便他们知道您正在遵守他们的行为条款。

接下来，您将需要访问 NASA 开放 API。 通过使用“DEMO_KEY”作为密钥，可以在没有 API 密钥的情况下使用 API。 例如：

唯一的限制是，使用演示密钥，您每小时只能发出 30 个请求，每个 IP 地址每天只能发出 50 个请求，而使用您的 API 密钥，您每小时最多可以发出 1000 个请求。 演示密钥为该项目提供了足够的请求，因为我每天只发出一个请求。 但我还是决定使用我的 API 密钥，因为我会在编写和测试代码时每小时发出多个请求。 此外，它很容易获得。

要获得对 NASA API 的访问权限：
转到api.nasa.gov 。 向下滚动页面，直到找到名为 Generate API Key 的表单。 填写您的个​​人信息并点击注册。 之后您应该会收到一个 API 密钥。

执行

先决条件

这是您将需要的三个库。
通过 HTTP请求从 NASA API 获取信息的请求，
您可以手动安装这些库，或者如果您克隆了我的 Github 存储库，请通过requirements.txt文件进行安装。

 pip install tweepy pip install requests pip install python-dotenv
 pip install requirements.txt

如果您使用的是 python-dotenv，则必须创建一个 .env 文件并编写您的 API 密钥和访问令牌。 例子：

 TWITTER_API_KEY = "YOUR_TWITTER_API_KEY" TWITTER_API_KEY_SECRET = "YOUR_TWITTER_API_KEY_SECRET" TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN = "YOUR_TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN" TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET = "YOUR_TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET" NASA_API_KEY = "YOUR_NASA_API_KEY"

从 NASA 的 API 获取内容

import requests from dotenv import load_dotenv import os load_dotenv () api_key = os . getenv ( 'NASA_API_KEY' ) json = requests . get ( f 'https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod?api_key= { api_key } ' ). json () if json [ 'media_type' ] == 'image' : title = json [ 'title' ] imageURL = json [ 'hdurl' ] content = Content ( title , imageURL )

在上面的代码中，我发送了一个 HTTP 请求并获取了一个 JSON，其中包含有关当天天文图片的信息。 下面是使用演示密钥的当今 JSON 的示例。 由此，我得到图像的标题和 URL。 有两个网址。 不同之处在于 hdurl 的分辨率更高。
我正在检查'media-type'的值是否为'image' ，因为有时 API 返回的是视频而不是图像。 在这种情况下，我会跳过那一天而不更新横幅。

下载图片

img_data = requests . get ( url ). content if not os . path . exists ( 'tmp' ): os . makedirs ( 'tmp' ) with open ( 'tmp/banner.jpg' , 'wb' ) as handler : handler . write ( img_data )

如果它不存在，我会在主目录中创建一个名为“tmp”的文件夹，我将在其中保存横幅图像。 我从从 NASA API 获得的 URL 获取图像。

在 Twitter API 上进行身份验证

import tweepy from dotenv import load_dotenv import os load_dotenv () api_key = os . getenv ( "TWITTER_API_KEY" ) api_key_secret = os . getenv ( "TWITTER_API_KEY_SECRET" ) access_token = os . getenv ( "TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN" ) access_token_secret = os . getenv ( "TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET" ) auth = tweepy . OAuthHandler ( api_key , api_key_secret ) auth . set_access_token ( access_token , access_token_secret ) api = tweepy . API ( auth )

使用 Tweepy 处理身份验证，我将我的 API 密钥和秘密传递给 Tweepy 的 OAuthHandler 对象，设置我的访问令牌，然后创建一个 API 实例。

更新个人资料

text = f 'Banner photo: { content . title } \n from: https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html' api . update_profile_banner ( filename = 'tmp/banner.jpg' ) api . update_profile ( description = text )

现在我已经使用 Tweepy 创建了一个 API 实例，从 NASA API 获取内容并下载了图像，我所要做的就是更新横幅。 我也在更新我的简历以显示图片的标题。

部署和设置调度程序

通过运行此代码，您的个人资料横幅和简历将被更新。 您必须每天运行它，因此您的个人资料每天都会更新。 我将代码部署在 Heroku 上，并使用调度程序每天运行主脚本。 首先，创建一个新应用程序。

在应用程序的部署选项卡上，选择您希望如何部署项目。 我将我的应用程序链接到 Github 存储库。 不过，如果项目不在 Github 上，您也可以使用 Heroku CLI 来部署项目。

在设置选项卡下，有一个字段可以添加您的配置变量。 您必须在此处添加您的 API 密钥、访问令牌和机密。 然后这些变量将变得可以访问，就像它们在 .env 文件中一样。

您可以打开右上角的控制台并运行项目的主脚本，以测试它是否通过在 Heroku 上运行将更改应用到您的 Twitter 个人资料。

接下来，您必须转到Addons 页面并查找名为 Heroku scheduler 的插件。 打开调度程序页面，然后单击安装。 您必须选择要使用此插件的项目。

要在 Heroku 上使用任何插件，您需要将您的信用卡信息添加到您的帐户账单设置中。 尽管如此，它不会向您收费，因为此插件是免费的，除非您每月多次运行该应用程序。

最后，返回您的应用概览选项卡。 您应该在已安装的插件下看到 Heroku 调度程序。 打开它并单击创建一个新工作。 然后，您必须填写要运行的命令以及它将运行的时间间隔。

结论

感谢您的阅读。 如果您有任何问题或建议，请在评论中告诉我。

这是 GitHub 存储库：

一旦暂停，viniciusenari 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，viniciusenari 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，viniciusenari 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 viniciusenari 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 viniciusenari：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 viniciusenari 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Vinicius Koji Enari

Posted on Aug 12

I find automating tasks one of the coolest things about programming. Lately, I've been thinking about ways to utilize APIs to automatize some things, and I came up with the idea of making my Twitter profile banner image change automatically from time to time. It would be like having access to a Twitter feature that no one has, the capability of having a slideshow as a banner instead of a single image.

I will use NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day API to obtain the images I will use. This API provides a different image daily, making it appropriate for this specific purpose. Also, by using this API, I know that my Twitter banner will repeat no pictures. You can use images from other APIs or your own.

Obtaining access to the APIs

Before starting, you'll need access to the Twitter API and the NASA API.

Twitter API

To access the Twitter API, you must go to Twitter's developers portal. Make sure to log into the portal with the same account you want your banner to change automatically. I'll not cover step-by-step what you need to do to obtain access to the Twitter API, but here is guide. You'll need to apply for Elevated Access to the API (step four) because the request to update one's profile is part of the v1.1 endpoints. It is not difficult to obtain elevated access. You'll have to explain how you will use the API so they know you are following their terms of conduct.

NASA's API

Next, you will need access to NASA Open APIs. NASA's APIs are free to use. It is possible to utilize the API without your API Key by utilizing "DEMO_KEY" as the key. For example:

https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod?api_key=DEMO_KEY

The only limitation is that with the demo key, you can only make 30 requests per hour and 50 requests per day per IP address, while with your API key, you can make up to 1000 requests per hour. The demo key provides enough requests for this project to work since I'm only making one request per day. But I decided to utilize my API key anyway because I would make multiple requests per hour while writing and testing the code. Also, it is easy to obtain.

To obtain access to the NASA API:
Go to api.nasa.gov. Scroll the page down until you find a form called Generate API Key. Fill in your personal information and click on signup. You should receive an API key right after.

Implementation

Prerequisites

The are three libraries that you will need.
Tweepy for more easily use of the Twitter API,
requests to obtain the information from the NASA API through an HTTP request,
python-dotenv, which I used to store and access my private keys without exposing them publicly.
You can manually install these libraries or if you cloned my Github repository, install through the requirements.txt file.

pip install tweepy
pip install requests
pip install python-dotenv

pip install requirements.txt

If you are using python-dotenv, you must create a .env file and write your API keys and access token. Example:

TWITTER_API_KEY="YOUR_TWITTER_API_KEY"
TWITTER_API_KEY_SECRET="YOUR_TWITTER_API_KEY_SECRET"
TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN="YOUR_TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN"
TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET="YOUR_TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET" NASA_API_KEY="YOUR_NASA_API_KEY"

Getting the content from NASA's API 

import requests
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os
load_dotenv() api_key = os.getenv('NASA_API_KEY') json = requests.get(f'https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod?api_key={api_key}').json() if json['media_type'] == 'image': title = json['title'] imageURL = json['hdurl'] content = Content(title, imageURL)

In the code above, I send an HTTP request and obtain a JSON containing information about today's astronomy picture of the day. Here is an example of what today's JSON looks like using the demo key. From this, I get the image's title and URL. There are two URLs. The difference is that hdurl is in a higher resolution.
I'm checking if the value of 'media-type' is 'image' because sometimes the API returns a video instead of an image. In that case, I'll skip that day and not update the banner.

Download the image 

img_data = requests.get(url).content
if not os.path.exists('tmp'): os.makedirs('tmp')
with open('tmp/banner.jpg', 'wb') as handler: handler.write(img_data)

If it does not exist, I create a folder called 'tmp' in the main directory, where I will save the banner image. I get the image from the URL I obtained from the NASA API.

Auth on Twitter API 

import tweepy
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import os
load_dotenv() api_key = os.getenv("TWITTER_API_KEY")
api_key_secret = os.getenv("TWITTER_API_KEY_SECRET")
access_token = os.getenv("TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN")
access_token_secret = os.getenv("TWITTER_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET") auth = tweepy.OAuthHandler(api_key, api_key_secret)
auth.set_access_token(access_token, access_token_secret)
api = tweepy.API(auth)

Using Tweepy to handle the authentication, I pass my API key and secret to Tweepy's OAuthHandler object, set my access token, and then create an API instance.

Updating profile 

text = f'Banner photo: {content.title}\nfrom: https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/astropix.html'
api.update_profile_banner(filename = 'tmp/banner.jpg')
api.update_profile(description = text)

Now that I have created an API instance using Tweepy, obtained the content from the NASA API, and downloaded the image, all I have to do is update the banner. I'm also updating my bio to display the title of the picture.

Deploying and setting a scheduler

By running this code, your profile banner and bio will be updated. You'll have to run it daily, so your profile is updated daily. I deployed the code on Heroku and used a scheduler to run the main script every day. Heroku allows you to have up to 5 free projects, so I did not have to pay to use this alternative.

First, create a new app.

On your app's deploy tab, choose how you want to deploy your project. I linked my app to the Github repository. Still, you can also use the Heroku CLI to deploy your project if it is not on Github.

Under the settings tabs, there is a field to add your config vars. You must add your API keys, access tokens, and secrets there. These variables will then become accessible as if they were in a .env file.

You can open the console on the top right and run the main script of your project to test if it applies the changes to your Twitter profile by running it on Heroku.

Next, you'll have to go to the Addons page and look for an addon called Heroku scheduler. Open the scheduler page and click on install. You'll have to select the project you want to use this addon.

To use any addon on Heroku, you need to have added your credit card info to your account billing settings. Still, it will not charge you since this addon is free unless you run the application many times a month.

Finally, go back to your app overview tab. You should see the Heroku scheduler under the installed addons. Open it and click on create a new job. You will then have to fill in what command you want to run and at what interval it will run.

Conclusion

Thank you for reading. Let me know in the comments if you have any questions or suggestions.

Here is the GitHub repository:

https://github.com/viniciusenari/twitter-profile-updater

Abdulrasaq Jamiu Adewuyi - Aug 6

Once suspended, viniciusenari will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, viniciusenari will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by viniciusenari will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If viniciusenari is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag viniciusenari:

viniciusenari consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging viniciusenari will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: viniciusenari

item.title: Forging GraphQL Bombs，Zip Bombs 的 2022 版本 | Forging GraphQL Bombs, the 2022 version of Zip Bombs
item.description:
本文最初发表在blog.escape.tech上。

实际上，您的 GraphQL 应用程序可能容易受到本文中我们称之为GraphQL Bombs的攻击。 继续阅读以了解您是否易受攻击以及如何保护您的 GraphQL 应用程序！

拉链炸弹是如何工作的？

在深入探讨这个话题之前，让我们花点时间了解一下拉链炸弹背后的概念。

它的工作原理是在数据中找到重复的模式，然后用更短的标记替换这些模式。

因此，重复的字节序列在压缩后会短得多。 创建一个 zip 炸弹包括精心制作一个压缩非常好的字节序列，压缩了几个数量级。 然后，当受害者解压缩 zip 文件时，生成的数据将比原始存档大得多。

别名查询

这些功能之一是别名查询的能力。

让我们考虑一个带有article(id: Int!)查询的简单博客。 如果我们要获取一篇文章，我们会这样做：

 query { article ( id : 1 ) { title author }
}

这将产生如下内容：

 { "article" : { "title" : "Hello World!" , "author" : "John Doe" }
}

但是 GraphQL 有一个很好的特性，允许开发人员使用不同的返回名称多次查询同一个解析器：

 query { first : article ( id : 1 ) { title author } second : article ( id : 2 ) { title author } third : article ( id : 3 ) { title author }
}

这将产生类似的结果，但article现在别名为first 、 second和third ：

 { "first" : { "title" : "Hello World!" , "author" : "John Doe" }, "second" : { "title" : "Yay, second article!" , "author" : "Jane Doe" }, "third" : { "title" : "That's a lot of articles" , "author" : "Jaune D'œuf" }
}

您可能已经注意到那里的一些东西，实际上，您已经可以设计第一个漏洞来利用此功能 -别名有助于蛮力：

 mutation { a1 : login ( user : "john" , password : "password" ) { id } a2 : login ( user : "john" , password : "qwerty" ) { id } a3 : login ( user : "john" , password : "123456" ) { id } # Let's try the most common passwords!
}

文件上传

该漏洞的第二部分需要通过 GraphQL 启用文件上传。

许多流行的 GraphQL 引擎支持在 GraphQL 中上传，有些甚至是原生的。

虽然通常的 GraphQL 查询是以application/json形式发送的，但文件上传是以multipart/form-data 。 这意味着 HTTP 请求体有多个部分，规范中描述的它们的功能可以总结如下：

  1. 操作部分包含 GraphQL 查询。 这是通常作为application/json发送的部分。
  2. 只要正确映射，任何其他部分都可以在操作部分中使用。 这些部分可能包含服务器可以处理的任何类型的数据，但通常是图​​像或二进制数据。

以下是个人资料图片上传的样子：

 POST /graphql HTTP / 1.1 Connection : keep-alive Content-Length : 78346 Content-Type : multipart/form-data; boundary=----boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry Host : example.com ------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry Content-Disposition: form-data; name="operations" { "query": "mutation ($picture: File!) {updateUserPicture(picture: $picture)}", "variables": { "picture": null } } ------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry Content-Disposition: form-data; name="map" { "file1": ["variables.picture"] } ------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file1"; filename="gautier.jpg" Content-Type: image/jpeg (77 kB of binary data) ------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry--

您可以在这里看到name="file1"的部分映射到variables.picture ，允许服务器在请求正文中找到文件。

至少从 HTTP 的角度来看，GraphQL 文件上传的工作方式与 REST 中的文件上传几乎相同，这意味着REST 中存在的漏洞可以在 GraphQL 中被利用。 但是，不幸的是，这还不是全部……

您可能已经猜到了，GraphQL 炸弹结合了本文中介绍的前两个功能。 概念如下：使用别名查询多次引用同一个文件。

假设我们调用updateUserPicture(picture: File!)一千次，使用别名，所有调用都引用同一个 1 MB 文件：

 mutation { a1 : updateUserPicture ( picture : $picture ) a2 : updateUserPicture ( picture : $picture ) a3 : updateUserPicture ( picture : $picture ) # ... a1000 : updateUserPicture ( picture : $picture )
}

此请求将小于 2 MB，但会产生 1 GB 的数据供服务器处理。

根据服务器对数据的处理方式，此请求可能会导致内存或 CPU 耗尽，从而导致性能下降甚至服务器崩溃。

减轻

缓解此漏洞需要几个步骤：



原文:

This article was originally published on blog.escape.tech.

Zip Bombs are a thing of the past, but the concept behind them is still relevant nowadays. Indeed, your GraphQL application might be vulnerable to what we'll call GraphQL Bombs in this article. Read on to know if you're vulnerable and how to secure your GraphQL application!

How do zip bombs work?

Before diving into the topic, let's take a moment to understand the concept behind zip bombs.

Zip files are lossless compressed archives whose most common compression algorithm is named deflate. It works by finding repeating patterns in the data, then replacing these patterns with a much shorter token.

Therefore, a repetitive sequence of bytes will be much shorter after compression. Creating a zip bomb consists of meticulously crafting a sequence of bytes that compress very well, by several orders of magnitude. Then, when the victim decompresses the zip file, the resulting data will be much larger than the original archive.

Aliased queries

GraphQL is a powerful language with many not-well-known features. One of these features is the ability to alias queries.

Let's consider a simple blog with an article(id: Int!) query. If we were to fetch one article, we would do it like this:

query { article(id: 1) { title author }
}

This will produce something like this:

{ "article": { "title": "Hello World!", "author": "John Doe" }
}

But GraphQL has a nice feature allowing developers to query the same resolver several times with a different return name:

query { first: article(id: 1) { title author } second: article(id: 2) { title author } third: article(id: 3) { title author }
}

This will produce a similar result, but article is now aliased as first, second and third:

{ "first": { "title": "Hello World!", "author": "John Doe" }, "second": { "title": "Yay, second article!", "author": "Jane Doe" }, "third": { "title": "That's a lot of articles", "author": "Jaune D'œuf" }
}

You may already notice something there, and indeed, you can already design a first vulnerability to exploit this feature – aliasing facilitates brute-force:

mutation { a1: login(user: "john", password: "password") { id } a2: login(user: "john", password: "qwerty") { id } a3: login(user: "john", password: "123456") { id } # Let's try the most common passwords!
}

File uploads

The second part of the vulnerability requires having enabled file uploads over GraphQL.

Many popular GraphQL engines support uploads in GraphQL, some even natively.

The GraphQL multipart specification describes how to implement file uploads in GraphQL. While usual GraphQL queries are sent as application/json, file uploads are sent as multipart/form-data. This means that the HTTP request body has multiple parts, and their functions, described in the specification, can be summarized as follows:

  1. The operations part contains the GraphQL query. This is the part that is usually sent as application/json.
  2. The map part helps the server to find data in the request body.
  3. Any other part can be used in the operations part as long as it is correctly mapped. These parts may contain any type of data the server can handle but are usually images or binary data.

Here is what a profile picture upload looks like:

POST /graphql HTTP/1.1
Connection: keep-alive
Content-Length: 78346
Content-Type: multipart/form-data; boundary=----boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry
Host: example.com

------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="operations"

{
  "query": "mutation ($picture: File!) {updateUserPicture(picture: $picture)}",
  "variables": { "picture": null }
}
------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="map"

{
  "file1": ["variables.picture"]
}
------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry
Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file1"; filename="gautier.jpg"
Content-Type: image/jpeg

(77 kB of binary data)
------boundaryMGv2RzA6GpOE3Hry--

You can see here that the part having name="file1" is mapped to variables.picture, allowing the server to find the file in the request body.

GraphQL file uploads work almost the same as file uploads in REST, at least from the HTTP perspective, which means that vulnerabilities that exist in REST can be exploited in GraphQL. But, unfortunately, that's not all…

GraphQL bombs

As you might have guessed, GraphQL bombs combine the two previous features introduced in the article. The concept is the following: referencing the same file several times using aliased queries.

Let's consider that we call updateUserPicture(picture: File!) one thousand times, using aliases, with all calls referencing the same 1 MB file:

mutation { a1: updateUserPicture(picture: $picture) a2: updateUserPicture(picture: $picture) a3: updateUserPicture(picture: $picture) # ... a1000: updateUserPicture(picture: $picture)
}

This request would be less than 2 MB but would result in 1 GB of data for the server to process.

Depending on what the server does with the data, this request may cause memory or CPU exhaustion, leading to decreased performance or even a server crash.

Mitigation

There are several steps necessary to mitigate this vulnerability:
item.author: gauben

item.title: AWS 中的低成本最佳实践 | Low-cost best practices in AWS
item.description:
你有没有觉得做正确的事情太昂贵了？ 好吧，你并没有完全错。 绝对最好的，最优秀的人类中最优秀的，最优秀的人能够让你的钱包显着流血，老实说，除了极少数用例外，它通常甚至不值得。

但这也不是做完全错误的事情的借口！ 有一个可以接受的中间点，在这里你投入一点就可以获得很多。 在本文中，我将向您展示您可以在 AWS 解决方案中执行的一些操作，以提高可靠性、性能效率、安全性、卓越运营或成本优化，或者可能不止一项。 它们不难，也不贵。 您将花费很少的时间和金钱，并从中获得很大的影响。 它们价格低廉！

实践：使用 MFA

涉及的 AWS 服务： IAM 或 IAM 身份中心

架构完善的支柱：安全性

描述：这很简单，总是使用 MFA。 可能的身份验证因素有：您拥有的东西（例如您的手机）、您知道的东西（例如您的密码）、您固有的东西（例如您的指纹）、您的位置。 实现此功能的一种简单方法是要求使用 MFA 设备，例如 Google Authenticator（适用于 Android 和 Iphone 的应用程序）进行登录。 这样，如果有人知道您的密码，他们将无法登录，他们也需要您的手机。

图片：

影响：

  • 如果有人知道您的密码，他们将无法仅凭密码登录，他们需要另一个因素。
  • 每次要登录时都必须从口袋里掏出手机可能有点烦人，但这比处理被劫持的帐户要烦人得多。

如何实施：前往 IAM 或 IAM 身份中心并为您的用户设置 MFA 设备。

一些提示：

  • 在 IAM 身份中心中，您可以要求用户设置 MFA 设备。
  • 在 IAM 策略中，您可以要求 MFA 执行某些操作，例如删除资源。

要避免的常见错误：

练习：单点登录的多账户设置

涉及的 AWS 服务： AWS Organizations、AWS IAM 身份中心

架构完善的支柱：卓越运营、安全性

说明：不要将所有 AWS 资源（用于开发和生产的 EC2 实例、用于备份的 S3 等）都放入同一个 AWS 账户。 相反，创建多个帐户（根据需要创建多个帐户），每个帐户都有明确定义的目的（例如应用程序 1 的开发环境），所有帐户都归入同一个组织。 使用合并帐单，因此您只需介绍一次付款详细信息。 使用 AWS IAM 身份中心（以前称为单点登录），这样您就可以为所有账户提供一个用户名 + 密码。 限制谁可以访问哪些帐户。

图片：

影响：

  • 工作负载分离和更容易的工作隔离
  • 能够引入服务控制策略（见下文）
  • 更好的访问控制
  • 没有长寿命的访问密钥

如何实施：创建一个新的 AWS 账户并使用它来创建一个 AWS 组织，启用所有功能。 即使您已经拥有 AWS 账户，也请执行此操作，并邀请您现有的 AWS 账户加入组织。 创建您需要的所有帐户（您可以稍后创建更多）。 转到 IAM 身份中心服务并在您的首选区域中进行设置。 如果您想使用现有的身份提供商，例如 Microsoft AD 或 Google Workspaces，请连接它。 否则，请直接在 IAM 身份中心中创建您的 SSO 用户。 将用户分组，为每个组分配权限。

一些提示：

  • 每个应用程序创建一个帐户，更好的是每个应用程序每个环境创建一个帐户（例如 App 1 Dev Env）。
  • 您可以将 AWS CLI 与 IAM 身份中心一起使用，请参阅此处如何配置它。
  • 您可以在 IAM 身份中心创建自己的用户，也可以连接现有的身份提供商，例如 Okta、Microsoft AD 或 Google Workspaces（以前称为 GSuite）。
  • 始终使用 MFA。
  • 多帐户设置中常见的一些帐户：安全性（用于访问日志和相关工具、设备等安全性内容）、共享服务（用于多个帐户、环境或应用程序的任何内容，例如 CI/CD )、日志存档（归档日志）、备份（如果他们在单独的帐户中，则更容易隔离它们并确保没有人具有写入或删除权限）、网络（共享网络服务，例如与所有帐户共享的 NAT 网关）。
  • 跨账户流量没有成本，并且可以与 Resource Access Manager 共享资源，例如，在属于另一个账户的子网中部署 EC2 实例。 对于其他内容，您需要由您的服务在另一个账户中承担 IAM 角色，并信任允许这样做的策略。

要避免的常见错误：

  • 从单个帐户迁移到多帐户设置时，请勿将现有帐户用作组织根。 在根帐户上部署资源不是一个好习惯，因为它不受服务控制策略的限制。 相反，创建一个新账户（您只需添加一次付款详细信息），使用它来创建 AWS 组织，然后邀请您现有的账户
  • 不要授予每个人访问所有内容的权限。 您的开发人员真的需要对生产帐户的写入权限吗？ 只授予必要的东西，并定期审查权限和必要性。

实践：服务控制策略

涉及的 AWS 服务： AWS Organizations

架构完善的支柱：安全性

说明：服务控制策略 (SCP) 类似于 IAM 策略，但它们适用于整个账户（或组织单元中的所有账户），并且它们不是实际授予权限，而是指定一个账户可以拥有的最大权限。 它们会影响根用户和每个 IAM 用户或角色，因此它们可用于阻止账户中的任何操作。 通常用作保障措施，例如拒绝您不使用的区域中的所有操作，或者仅在资源具有特定标签时才允许创建资源。

图片：

影响：

  • 围绕人们可以在帐户中做什么的护栏
  • 能够拒绝对每个用户（包括 root 用户）执行操作

如何实施：在您的组织的根帐户上，转到组织服务并确保为您的组织启用了所有功能。 转到策略和服务控制策略。 编写一份政策或从官方示例政策或其他良好资源中复制一份。 然后转到帐户，选择一个帐户或组织单位，单击策略选项卡并将您的新策略附加到帐户/OU。

一些提示：

要避免的常见错误：

  • 如果 SCP 未明确允许某个操作（例如，您删除了允许所有操作的默认 SCP），则该操作将被拒绝。
  • 因此，该帐户比其他帐户更容易受到攻击。 最佳实践是尽可能少地使用 root 帐户，并且永远不要在那里部署工作负载。
  • 允许每个人访问组织的 root 帐户，以便他们可以修改 SCP，有助于防止错误和事故，但不会保护您免受攻击者的侵害。

练习：加密您的 EBS 卷

涉及的 AWS 服务： AWS Key Management System、AWS Elastic Block Storage

架构完善的支柱：安全性

描述：静态数据应该被加密。 创建 EBS 卷时，您只需将下拉菜单从“否”更改为“是”即可对其进行加密。 很简单，对吧？ 好吧，你可能会忘记。 老实说，你会在某个时候忘记。 幸运的是，您可以选择默认启用加密，这意味着所有 EBS 卷将始终被加密。 启用非常简单，但请记住，它是在每个区域的基础上启用的，并且用于加密的 KMS 密钥是区域性的。

图片：

影响：

  • 您的所有 EBS 卷都将被加密
  • 您的所有 EBS 快照也将使用用于卷的相同 KMS 密钥进行加密。 这意味着要与另一个帐户共享快照，您需要授予该帐户使用 KMS 密钥的权限。
  • 您将无法公开共享加密的 AMI，并且您跨账户共享的任何 AMI 都需要访问您选择的 KMS 密钥。
  • 如果您使用 AWS 托管 CMK 进行加密，您将无法共享快照/AMI
  • 默认加密设置是按区域的。 如何实现：打开 EC2 控制台。 在 EC2 仪表板上向下滚动到 Account attributes，然后单击 EBS 加密。 单击管理，勾选始终加密新 EBS 卷下方的框，然后单击更新 EBS 加密。 而已。

    一些提示：

    • 您可以为不同的卷或工作负载使用不同的 KMS 密钥，并且可以对这些 KMS 密钥使用策略来控制谁可以访问卷及其快照
    • 当您复制快照（在同一区域或跨区域）时，您可以指定一个新的 KMS 密钥对其进行加密
    • 要避免的常见错误：

      包起来

      这只是一个良好实践的列表，您可以用很少的时间和金钱投资实施这些实践，并产生巨大的影响。 成本低，物超所值/时间。

      这绝不是一份详尽的清单。 这可能是一个很好的起点，但不要止步于此。 不断寻找容易实现的目标、可以改进的简单事物，并不断改进您的云解决方案。

      您可能不需要大型且超级昂贵的解决方案。 不要过度支付，不要过度设计。 但不要安定下来。

      自我推销

      我为初创公司创建可扩展、安全且具有成本效益的云解决方案。 这意味着应用这些以及更多最佳实践，重点是提高解决方案的技术卓越性，而不是让您为此付出很多钱。 我还可以为您提供一些更复杂的解决方案，但前提是您确实需要它们。

      如果您需要最佳实践，无论您是刚刚开始还是需要解决某些问题，请给我打电话。 如果您需要更复杂的东西，也请给我打电话，我会帮助您确定您需要走多远，并构建最简单的解决方案，将您带到那里。

一旦暂停，aws-builder 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，aws-builders 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，aws-builders 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 aws-builders 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 aws-builders：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 aws-builders 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Have you ever felt that doing things right is way too expensive? Well, you're not entirely wrong. The absolute best, bleeding edge best of the best of the best humanity is capable of will make your wallet bleed significantly, and to be honest it's often not even worth it except for very few use cases.

But that's no excuse to doing things completely wrong either! There is an acceptable middle point, where you invest a little to get a lot. In this article I'm going to show you a few things you can do in your AWS solution to improve your Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Security, Operational Excellence or Cost Optimization, or perhaps more than one of those. They're not hard and they're not expensive. You'll spend very little time and money, and get a big impact out of this. They're low-cost!

Practice: Use MFA

AWS services involved: IAM or IAM Identity Center

Well-Architected pillars: Security

Description: It's pretty simple, always use MFA. MFA stands for Multi-Factor Authentication. Possible authentication factors are: Something that you possess (e.g. your phone), something that you know (e.g. your password), something that is inherent to you (e.g. your fingerprints), your location. A simple way to implement this is to require an MFA device such as Google Authenticator (an app for Android and Iphone) for logins. That way, if someone knows your password they won't be able to log in, they'd need your phone as well.

Picture:

Impact:

  • If someone knows your password they won't be able to log in with just that, they'd need another factor.
  • It can be a bit annoying to have to pull your phone out of your pocket every time you want to log in, but it's a lot less annoying than dealing with a hijacked account.

How to implement: Go to IAM or IAM Identity Center and set up your MFA device for your user.

A few tips:

  • In IAM Identity Center you can require that users have an MFA device set up.
  • In IAM Policies you can require MFA for some actions, such as deleting resources.

Common mistakes to avoid:

Practice: Multi-account setup with single sign-on

AWS services involved: AWS Organizations, AWS IAM Identity Center

Well-Architected pillars: Operational Excellence, Security

Description: Don't throw every AWS resource (EC2 instances for dev and prod, S3 for backups, etc) into the same AWS account. Instead, create multiple accounts (as many as you need), each with a clearly defined purpose (e.g. dev environment for app 1), all grouped under the same Organization. Use consolidated billing so you only need to introduce your payment details once. Use AWS IAM Identity Center (formerly Single Sign-On) so you have just one username+password for all accounts. Limit who can access which accounts.

Picture:

Impact:

  • Workload separation and much easier work isolation
  • Ability to introduce Service Control Policies (see below)
  • Better access control
  • No long-lived access keys

How to implement: Create a new AWS account and use it to create an AWS Organization, enabling all features. Do this even if you already have an AWS account, and invite your existing AWS account into the Organization. Create all accounts that you need (you can create more later). Go to the IAM Identity Center service and set it up in your preferred region. If you want to use an existing identity provider such as Microsoft AD or Google Workspaces, connect it. Otherwise, create your SSO users directly in IAM Identity Center. Group users in groups, assign permissions to each group.

A few tips:

  • Create one account per app, and even better one account per environment per app (e.g. App 1 Dev Env).
  • You can use AWS CLI with IAM Identity Center, see here how to configure it.
  • You can create your own users in IAM Identity Center or you can connect your existing identity provider such as Okta, Microsoft AD or Google Workspaces (formerly GSuite).
  • Always use MFA.
  • There are a few accounts that are common in multi-account setups: Security (for security stuff such as access logs and related tools, appliances, etc), Shared Services (anything that's for multiple accounts, environments or apps, such as CI/CD), Log Archive (archiving logs), Backups (it's easier to isolate them and ensure nobody has write or delete access if they're in a separate account), Networking (shared network services, for example NAT Gateways shared with all accounts).
  • There's no cost for cross-account traffic, and resources can be shared with Resource Access Manager to, for example, deploy an EC2 instance in a subnet that belongs to another account. For other stuff you'll need IAM roles to be assumed by your services in another account and trust policies that permit that.

Common mistakes to avoid:

  • When migrating from a single account to a multi-account setup, don't use your existing account as the Organization root. It's not a good practice to deploy resources on the root account, because it can't be limited by Service Control Policies. Instead, create a new account (you'll need to add payment details just this once), use it to create an AWS Organization and then invite your existing account
  • Don't grant everyone access to everything. Do your developers really need write access to the production account? Grant only what's necessary, and review the permissions and the necessities periodically.

Practice: Service Control Policies

AWS services involved: AWS Organizations

Well-Architected pillars: Security

Description: Service Control Policies (SCPs) are like IAM Policies, but they apply to the whole account (or all accounts in the Organizational Unit), and instead of actually granting permissions, they specify the maximum permissions an account can have. SCPs require the account to be in an Organization (see above). They affect the root user and every IAM user or role, so they can be used to prevent any action in an account. Typically used as safeguards, e.g. deny all actions in a region you don't use, or only allow creating resources if they have a certain tag.

Picture:

Impact:

  • Guardrails around what people can do in an account
  • Ability to deny actions to every user, including the root user

How to implement: On the root account of your organization go to the Organizations service and make sure that all features are enabled for your organization. Go to Policies and Service Control Policies. Write a policy or copy one from the official example policies or other good sources. Then go to Accounts, choose an account or organizational unit, click on the Policies tab and attach your new policy to the account/OU.

A few tips:

Common mistakes to avoid:

  • If an action is not explicitly allowed by an SCP (for example you deleted the default SCP that allows all actions), then it's denied.
  • SCPs do not apply at all to the root account of the organization. For that reason, that account is more vulnerable than the other ones. The best practice is to use the root account as little as possible, and never deploy workloads there.
  • Allowing everyone access to the root account of the organization, so that they can modify SCPs, can help prevent mistakes and accidents, but won't protect you from an attacker.

Practice: Encrypt your EBS volumes

AWS services involved: AWS Key Management System, AWS Elastic Block Storage

Well-Architected pillars: Security

Description: Data at rest should be encrypted. When you create an EBS volume you have the option to encrypt it just by changing a dropdown from No to Yes. Simple enough, right? Well, you might forget. Let's be honest, you will forget, at some point. Luckily, you have the option to enable encryption by default, which means that all EBS volumes will always be encrypted. It's pretty simple to enable, but keep in mind that it's enabled on a per-region basis and that the KMS keys used for encryption are regional.

Picture:

Impact:

  • All your EBS volumes will be encrypted
  • All your EBS snapshots will also be encrypted, with the same KMS key used for the volume. This means that to share a snapshot with another account you will need to grant that account permissions to use the KMS key.
  • You won’t be able to share encrypted AMIs publicly, and any AMIs you share across accounts need access to your chosen KMS key.
  • You won’t be able to share snapshots / AMI if you encrypt with AWS managed CMK
  • The default encryption settings are per-region. As are the KMS keys.
  • Amazon EBS does not support asymmetric CMKs.

How to implement: Open the EC2 console. On the EC2 Dashboard scroll down to Account attributes and click EBS encryption. Click Manage, tick the box below Always encrypt new EBS volumes and click Update EBS Encryption. That's it.

A few tips:

  • You can use different KMS keys for different volumes or workloads, and you can use policies on those KMS keys to control who can access the volumes and their snapshots
  • When you copy a snapshot (in the same region or across regions) you can specify a new KMS key to encrypt it
  • You can use multi-region KMS keys

Common mistakes to avoid:

Wrapping up

This was just a list of good practices that you can implement for a really small time and money investment, and get a big impact. Low cost, and best value for money/time.

It's by no means an exhaustive list. It could be a good starting point, but don't stop here. Keep finding the low hanging fruit, the simple things you can improve, and keep improving your cloud solution.

You probably don't need big and super expensive solutions. Don't overpay, don't overengineer. But don't settle.

Self promotion

I create scalable, secure and cost-efficient cloud solutions for startups. This means applying these and many more best practices, with a focus on both improving the technical excellence of your solution and not making you pay a lot of money to do so. I can also offer you some more complicated solutions, but only if you really need them.

If you need best practices, whether you're just starting or you need to fix something, give me a call. If you need something more complex, give me a call as well, I'll help you figure out how far you need to go and build the simplest solution that takes you there.

Davide 'CoderDave' Benvegnù - Jun 14

Once suspended, aws-builders will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, aws-builders will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by aws-builders will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If aws-builders is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag aws-builders:

aws-builders consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging aws-builders will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: guilleojeda

item.title: 如何建立自己的个人博客：分步指南 | How to set up your own personal blog: Step-By-Step Guide
item.description:
拉克什·波特努鲁

发表于我发表了一篇关于我如何构建博客以及为什么使用特定技术的文章。 许多人喜欢它并想开始自己的博客。 因此，如果您想在不使用第三方平台的情况下创建个人博客，请遵循本教程。
这篇文章将引导您设置您自己的我的博客实例。 我将介绍从 SEO 到托管和 Google AdSense 设置的所有内容。 不用担心，用于开发本网站的所有第三方工具和服务都有免费计划。

拥有个人博客的好处

  • 完全控制您的内容。
  • 添加您自己的功能。
  • 随心所欲地通过您的内容获利。

让我们开始吧

本博客的主要特点

  1. 帖子 - 文章/教程/指南
  2. 片段 - 迷你博客
  3. 搜索，
    • 主搜索 - 允许用户搜索任何内容的中央搜索功能。
    • 搜索页面 - 允许访问者查找特定内容的类别搜索。
  4. 音频帖子 - 添加音频文章
  5. 评论系统 - 一个 GitHub 驱动的评论系统
  6. 货币化，
    • 网络货币化——人们可以通过阅读他们的内容来帮助创作者。
    • 分析 - 谷歌分析可以帮助您了解访问者如何与您的网站和内容互动。
    • 完全响应。
    • 在我们开始之前
      • 此博客尚未经过彻底测试。
      • 某些页面在屏幕分辨率较低的设备上可能无法响应。
      • 如果您对设置有任何疑问，请在此处开始讨论或在过去的讨论中寻找答案。

      先决条件

      • 只要你不想修改源代码，只需要一点开发经验（能够按照本文提供的步骤）就可以搭建这个博客。

      让我们开始吧

      创建或登录您的Hygraph帐户。

    • 单击下面的按钮克隆我的 Hygraph 项目。

    • 创建或登录您的Cloudinary帐户。
    • 按照本指南在 Hygraph 中安装 Cloudinary UI 扩展。

    您现在可以开始添加内容了。

    在 freeCodeCamp News 上阅读我写的这篇文章来设置 giscus。 复制生成的script标签并将其记在某处。

  7. 打开giscus.json文件并将我的站点 URL 替换为您的站点 URL。

    8. 谷歌分析设置

    • 创建或登录您的Google Analytics（分析）帐户。
    • 为您的博客创建一个 GA4 属性。
    • 复制您的属性测量 ID 并在某处注明。

    线圈设置

    您可以在线圈上注册为创建者并将网络货币化标签添加到您的网站。

    • 创建或登录您的Coil帐户。
    • 将您的 Uphold 帐户连接到线圈以在您的 Uphold 钱包中接收付款。
    • 导航到“设置”->“支付”。
    • 复制“PAYMENT POINTER”并记下。 您的付款指针应该是这样的。
     $ilp .uphold.com/82eq8NKY4NEZ

    如果您想在帖子中添加音频文章，请创建一个 BeyondWords 帐户，否则请跳过此部分。

    • 创建或登录您的BeyondWords帐户。
    • 创建一个项目并开始创建音频。
    • 您可以从“可共享 URL”中获取音频 ID。
     https://audio.beyondwords.io/play/AUDIO_ID

    它比 BuyMeACoffee 有更多好处。

    • 创建或登录您的ko-fi帐户。
    • 导航到按钮和小部件页面 -> 单击“Ko-fi 捐赠小部件” -> 选择“捐赠面板”。
    • 复制嵌入 URL 并在某处记下它。 通讯设置

    我使用 substack 作为我的时事通讯服务。 您可以使用任何您想要的服务。 将 URL 放入此环境变量中。

     NEXT_PUBLIC_SUBSTACK_URL =

    前端设置

    现在我们已经准备好后端 CMS。 让我们设置前端。

    • 打开博客 GitHub 存储库。
    • 给它加星⭐。
    • 分叉回购。
    • 克隆你的分叉回购。
     git clone https://github.com/[YOUR_USERNAME]/[YOUR_FORKED_PROJECT_NAME].git
    • 在代码编辑器或 IDE 中打开克隆的 repo。
    • 打开public文件夹，
      • 用你的图标替换我的图标。 使用它来生成图标。
      • 打开manifest.json文件并将我的信息替换为您的信息。
    • 打开config/site.config.js文件并将我的信息替换为您的信息。 将更改推送到您的 GitHub 存储库。

    部署到 Netlify

    • 创建或登录您的Netlify帐户。
    • 单击“添加新站点”-> 选择“导入现有项目”。
    • 选择“GitHub”并授权 Netlify 连接到 GitHub。
    • 搜索您的存储库并选择它。
    • 在站点设置中，将所有内容保留为默认值，然后单击“显示高级”按钮。
    • 单击“新变量”并参考.env.example文件。 您必须在 Netlify 项目中添加所有环境变量。
    • 添加所有变量后，单击“部署站点”。
    • 添加自定义域或使用 Netlify 子域。

    谷歌 AdSense 设置

    • 创建或登录您的Google AdSense帐户。
    • 通过单击“添加站点”添加您的站点。
    • 等待一两天让您的网站被接受。

    如果接受，请按照以下步骤创建广告单元。

    • 导航到“设置”->“帐户信息”-> 复制“发布者 ID”并将其粘贴到site.config.js文件中。
    • 转到“广告”->“按广告单元”。
    • 点击“展示广告”。 将您的广告命名为“页顶广告”-> 点击“创建”。 复制data-ad-slot值并粘贴到site.config.js文件中。
    • 与此相同，创建“In-feed Ad”并在site.config.js中添加槽号。
    • 按照此操作将ads.txt文件添加到public中。 如果已经存在，请将其替换为您的ads.txt文件。

    贡献

    喜欢添加功能？ 发现错误？ 或者想要改进代码库？ 而已！ 如果您在设置时遇到任何问题，请发起讨论，我会在那里为您提供帮助。

    在评论中分享您的博客链接👇。 关注更多💖。

一旦暂停，itsrakesh 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，itsrakesh 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，itsrakesh 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 itsrakesh 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 itsrakesh 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 itsrakesh 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Rakesh Potnuru

Posted on Aug 12 • Originally published at blog.itsrakesh.co

I published an article about how I built my blog and why I used specific technology. Many folks enjoyed it and wanted to start their own blogs. So, if you want to start a personal blog without using third-party platforms, follow this tutorial.
This post will walk you through setting up your own instance of my blog. I'll go over everything from SEO to hosting and Google AdSense set up. Don't worry, all of the third-party tools and services used to develop this site have free plans.

Benefits of having a personal blog

  • Full control over your content.
  • Add your own features.
  • Monetize your content however you want.

Let's get started

Main features of this blog

  1. Posts - Articles/Tutorials/Guides
  2. Snippets - Mini blogs
  3. Search,
    • Main search - A central search function that allows users to search for anything.
    • Search page - A category search that allows visitors to look for specific content.
  4. Audio posts - Add audio articles
  5. Comment system - A GitHub-powered comment system
  6. Ko-fi - The embed contribution widget allows your fans to donate to you without leaving your website.
  7. Monetization,
    • Web monetization - People can help creators by just reading their content.
    • AdSense - Earn revenue by displaying ads.
  8. Analytics - Google analytics can help you learn how visitors interact with your website and content.
  9. Fully responsive.
  10. 100 SEO score.

Before we start

  • This blog has not been thoroughly tested.
  • Some pages may not be responsive on devices with low screen resolution.
  • If you have any questions about the setup, please start a discussion here or look for answers in past discussions.

Prerequisites

  • As long as you don't want to make changes to the source code, you only need a little development experience (able to follow the steps provided in this article) to set up this blog.

Let's get started

Hygraph (Previously GraphCMS) project set up

  • Create or sign in to your Hygraph account.
  • Click the button below to clone my Hygraph project.

  • Create or sign in to your Cloudinary account.
  • Follow this guide to installing the Cloudinary UI extension in Hygraph.

You can now start adding content.

Giscus set up

Giscus is a comment system powered by GitHub Discussions.

  • Read this article written by me on freeCodeCamp News to set up giscus. (Read till section "How to Integrate giscus in Your Blog")
  • Copy the generated script tag and note it somewhere.
  • Open the giscus.json file and replace my site URL with your site URL.

Google Analytics set up

  • Create or sign in to your Google Analytics account.
  • Create a GA4 property for your blog.
  • Copy your property measurement id and note it somewhere.

Coil set up

Coil is a web monetization provider. You can sign up on coil as a creator and add the web monetization tag to your site.

  • Create or sign in to your Coil account.
  • Connect your Uphold account to the coil to receive payouts on your Uphold wallet.
  • Navigate to "Settings" -> "Payouts".
  • Copy "PAYMENT POINTER" and note it. Your payment pointer should be something like this. 
$ilp.uphold.com/82eq8NKY4NEZ

BeyondWords set up

If you want to add audio articles to your posts, then create a BeyondWords account otherwise just skip this section.

  • Create or sign in to your BeyondWords account.
  • Create a project and start creating audios.
  • You can get an audio Id from the "Shareable URL". 
https://audio.beyondwords.io/play/AUDIO_ID

Ko-fi set up

Ko-fi is a platform that enables you to accept donations, memberships, and sales from your support. It has a few more benefits over BuyMeACoffee.

  • Create or sign in to your ko-fi account.
  • Navigate to Buttons & Widgets page -> Click on "Ko-fi Donation Widget" -> Select "Donation Panel".
  • Copy the embed URL and note it somewhere. The URL should look like this. 
https://ko-fi.com/YOUR_KOFI_USERNAME/?hidefeed=true&widget=true&embed=true&preview=true

Newsletter set up

I use substack as my newsletter service. You can use whichever service you wish. Put the URL in this env variable.

NEXT_PUBLIC_SUBSTACK_URL=

Frontend set up

Now that we have backend CMS ready. Let's set up the front end.

  • Open Blog GitHub repo.
  • Star it ⭐.
  • Fork the repo.
  • Clone your forked repo. 
git clone https://github.com/[YOUR_USERNAME]/[YOUR_FORKED_PROJECT_NAME].git
  • Open the cloned repo in your code editor or IDE.
  • Open the public folder,
    • Replace my icons with your icons. Use this to generate icons.
    • Open the manifest.json file and replace my info with your info.
  • Open config/site.config.js file and replace my info with your info. (Don't worry if you cannot fill in some details. We will cover them later in this article.)
  • Push changes to your GitHub repo.

Deploy to Netlify

  • Create or sign in to your Netlify account.
  • Click on "Add new site" -> Select "Import an existing project".
  • Select "GitHub" and authorize Netlify to connect to GitHub.
  • Search for your repo and select it.
  • In the site settings, leave everything default and click the "Show advanced" button.
  • Click on "New variable" and refer to the .env.example file. You have to add all the env variables in your Netlify project.
  • After adding all the variables, click "Deploy site".
  • Add a custom domain or just go with the Netlify subdomain.

Google AdSense set up

  • Create or sign in to your Google AdSense account.
  • Add your site by clicking "Add Site".
  • Wait a day or two to get your site accepted.

If accepted, follow these steps to create ad units.

  • Navigate to "Settings" -> "Account information" -> copy "Publisher ID" and paste it in site.config.js file.
  • Go to "Ads" -> "By ad unit".
  • Click on "Display ad". Name your ad "Page top Ad" -> Click "Create". Copy data-ad-slot value and paste in site.config.js file.
  • Same as this, create "In-feed Ad" and add slot number in site.config.js.
  • Follow this to add ads.txt file in public folder. If one already exists, please replace it with your ads.txt file.

Contribute

Like to add a feature? Found a bug? or Want to improve the codebase? - Open an issue.

That's it! If you are facing any trouble setting up, please start a discussion, I will be there to assist you.

Share your blog link in the comments 👇. Follow for more 💖.

Once suspended, itsrakesh will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, itsrakesh will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by itsrakesh will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If itsrakesh is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag itsrakesh:

itsrakesh consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging itsrakesh will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: itsrakesh

item.title: NFT 市场中 ERC-2981 的必然性 | The Inevitability of ERC-2981 in NFT Marketplaces
item.description:
它是 Web3 的 Todo App 等价物。 它已成为当今 Web3 面试中不言而喻的标准之一。

今年早些时候，我们看到了没有内在价值的像素化图像的兴起，我们看到了百万富翁的诞生。 考虑推出代表其品牌的自己的 NFT 收藏品的 IT 公司和特许经营者是谁。

在所有这些中，有一件小事被许多开发人员忽视了，那就是版税标准的引入。 在撰写本文时， NFT 版税标准已经快 2 岁了。

大多数时候，您可能会发现人们使用 Opensea，甚至通过分叉 Seaport 来建立自己的市场。 但是偶尔会有一位喜欢从头开始的铁杆开发人员。 本文旨在帮助 Web3 开发人员（无论是初出茅庐的老手）了解它。 绝不提倡在面试问题中包含 ERC-2981，哈哈。

前提

如果你仔细想想，NFT 版税标准并不是像分数 NFT 和 NFT 流动性那样的任何前沿自以为是的想法。 这是一个相当简单的论点——如果你正在为其他人设计一个市场，他们有什么动机在上面出售他们的 NFT？

我的意思是，他们最初可以卖掉它，就是这样。 但是，如果该代币的价值可笑地升值而原始创建者却看不到任何大笔钱，会发生什么？ 因此，NFT 版税标准就像是对内容创作者的致敬，并帮助他们在每次作品易手时分一杯羹。 在某种程度上，它成为被动收入的来源。 现在，这类功能将使 NFT 市场真正有利可图。

它的代码...

在撰写本文时，Openzeppelin 已经有了 ERC-2981 的可靠实现，尽管它可能没有直接列在文档中。 它虽然提供服务作为扩展。 您可以在Github上浏览 NFT 版税标准的 Openzeppelin 实现代码。

有趣的是，像这样的标准只引入了一种方法royaltyInfo(uint256 tokenId, uint256 salePrice) 。 这是为了提供需要作为版税转让给原始创建者的可替代代币的数量。

您可以尝试仅使用 Openzeppelin 提供的ERC-2981 接口来实现自己的 ERC-2981。 但用轮子造车总是比重新发现轮子更明智。

代码提出的想法很简单——你维护一个映射，从 NFT 的 ID 和将版税发送到的地址以及构成版税的原始价格的一部分。 这就是ERC2981 合约中的以下代码的作用。

 struct RoyaltyInfo { address receiver ; uint96 royaltyFraction ; } RoyaltyInfo private _defaultRoyaltyInfo ; mapping ( uint256 => RoyaltyInfo ) private _tokenRoyaltyInfo ;

请记住，上述代码是抽象合约的一部分。 因此，您必须自己实现任何缺少的方法（或者您可以使用 ERC721 扩展）。

每次royaltyInfo(uint256 _tokenId, uint256 _salePrice)函数时，它只会在_tokenRoyaltyInfo映射中进行查找，并计算_salePrice的哪一部分将发送到上述代码块内结构中记录为接收者的地址。

如果您想为所有代币设置一个固定的版税分数，那么就不要碰这个功能。 相反，只需使用_setDefaultRoyalty(address receiver, uint96 feeNumerator)进行设置。 请记住， receiver参数在这里基本上无关紧要。 每个 Solidity 开发人员都应该知道，任何未初始化的地址变量都将具有零地址（ 0x0 ）的默认值。 合约中的royaltyInfo(uint256 _tokenId, uint256 _salePrice)函数检查是否存在除指定为给定tokenId接收方的零地址以外的任何地址。 如果没有地址，它会自动使用默认的分数值来计算 NFT 的版税。

请记住，本合同仅计算版税。 它不会将令牌发送给内容创建者/接收者。 在 NFT 转移期间，您需要在合约中手动处理该逻辑。

结论

容易，对吧？ 但它在生态系统中引入的想法是将普通 NFT 市场与好的市场区分开来，并且是决定谁领导采用竞赛的黑马之一。 下次您发现自己在实施 Marketplace 时，请考虑将这个秘诀添加到其中。

这就是本文的内容。 如果您认为我错过了什么，请随时在下面的评论中指出。 如果您认为有什么值得讨论的，请发表评论。 如果您喜欢阅读这篇文章，请考虑发表评论并关注我以获取更多此类内容。 直到下一次，继续在 Web3 和 WAGMI 上构建很棒的东西！



原文:

NFT Marketplace is probably one those projects which every beginner makes. It is the Todo App equivalent of Web3. An NFT marketplace encompasses a whole of topics which prove grasp over the development of Dapps - from designing custom ERC20 contract to act as currency to Cross-contract invocations. It has become one of the unspoken standard in Web3 interviews now-a-days.

Earlier this year we saw the rise of pixelated images with no intrinsic value and we saw millionaires being made out of them. NFTs certainly have become a household name. Who's who of IT firms and franchises considering launching their own NFT collections representing their Brand.

Amidst all these there is one of those small things which has gone unnoticed by many developers and that's the introduction of Royalty standards. At the time of writing NFT Royalty Standard is almost 2 years old.

Most of the time you might find people using Opensea or even building their own marketplace by forking Seaport. But once in a while, there comes one of those hardcore developers who likes to do it from scratch. This article is supposed to help Web3 developers (both budding and unaware veterans) get to know about it. It is no way advocating the inclusion of ERC-2981 in interview questions, lol.

The Premise

If you think about it, NFT Royalty Standard ain't any cutting edge opinionated idea like fractional NFTs and NFT liquidity. It is rather a simple argument - if you are designing a Marketplace for others, what incentive would they have to sell their NFTs on it?

I mean sure, they could sell it off initially and that's that. But what happens if that token appreciates in value ridiculously and the original creator gets to see none of the big bucks? The NFT Royalty Standard is, thus, like a tribute to the content creators and helps them get a piece of the action every time their work changes hands. It, in a way, becomes a source of passive income. Now something of that sort is a feature that would make NFT marketplaces really lucrative.

Itsy-bitsy code...

At the time of writing, Openzeppelin already has a solid implementation of ERC-2981 though it might not be listed on the Documentation directly. It provides the service as an extension though. You can browse the code for the Openzeppelin implementation of NFT Royalty Standard on Github.

Funnily enough a standard like only introduces one method - royaltyInfo(uint256 tokenId, uint256 salePrice). This is meant to give the amount of fungible tokens that need to be transferred to the original creator as a royalty.

You can try to have your own implementation of ERC-2981 by using just the ERC-2981 Interface provided by Openzeppelin. But it is always wiser to use the wheel to build a car than to rediscover the wheel.

The idea presented by the code is simple - you maintain a mapping from an NFT's Id and the address to send the royalty to along with the fraction of the original price that constitutes the royalty. That is what the code below from the ERC2981 contract does.

 struct RoyaltyInfo { address receiver; uint96 royaltyFraction; } RoyaltyInfo private _defaultRoyaltyInfo; mapping(uint256 => RoyaltyInfo) private _tokenRoyaltyInfo;

Keep in mind that the above code is part of an abstract contract. So you will have to implement any missing methods on your own (or you could go for the ERC721 extension).

Everytime the royaltyInfo(uint256 _tokenId, uint256 _salePrice) function gets called, it just performs a lookup in the _tokenRoyaltyInfo mapping and calculates what fraction of _salePrice will be sent to the address recorded as the receiver in the struct inside the above codeblock.

The _setTokenRoyalty(uint256 tokenId, address receiver, uint96 feeNumerator) function can be used to manually set the royalty fraction for a specific token.

If you want to have a fixed royalty fraction for all tokens, then just don't touch this function. Instead just set it up using the _setDefaultRoyalty(address receiver, uint96 feeNumerator). Keep in mind that the receiver argument essentially does not matter here. Every solidity developer should know that any uninitialized address variable will have a default value of Zero address (0x0). The royaltyInfo(uint256 _tokenId, uint256 _salePrice) function in the contract checks if there is any address other than the Zero address specified as the receiver for the given tokenId. In case there is no address, it automatically uses the default fraction value to calculate the NFT's Royalty.

Keep in mind that this contract only calculates the royalty. It does not send the tokens to the content creator/receiver. You need to handle that logic manually in your contract during the NFT transfer.

Conclusion

Easy, right? ERC-2981 ain't that tough. But the idea it introduced in the ecosystem is what differentiates common NFT marketplaces from the good ones and is one of those dark horses which determine who leads the adoption race. Next time you find yourself implementing a Marketplace, consider adding this recipe to it.

That's it for this article. If you think I missed something, feel free to point it out in the comments below. If you think there is something that warrants discussion, leave comments. If you liked reading the article, consider leaving a reaction and following me for more such content. Until next time, keep building awesome stuff on Web3 and WAGMI!
item.author: abhikbanerjee99

item.title: 为 Google Home 打造“智能设备” | Build your «Smart device» for Google Home
item.description:
我们将使用云到云的集成方法。 所以我们需要导航到Google 控制台上的 Actions并创建一个新项目：


我们需要将我们的智能家居行动命名为：


设置 OAuth2 和后端服务器

云到云集成需要OAuth2服务器才能将您的智能设备服务链接到 Google Home 应用程序。 在本文中，我们将使用Laravel 框架和Passport 包来实现它。 您应该通过基本步骤，例如Laravel和Passport安装、数据库设置以及创建假用户，或者您可以在此处找到示例代码。 接下来将描述所有附加动作。

帐号关联

移动到您的 Laravel 项目并运行命令为 Google Home 生成 OAuth 客户端信息。

 $ php artisan passport:client Which user ID should the client be assigned to?: > 1 What should we name the client?: > Google Where should we redirect the request after authorization?: > https://oauth-redirect.googleusercontent.com/r/ { your project id } New client created successfully. Client ID: 9700039b-92b7-4a79-a421-152747b9a257 Client secret: 813PEwdTAq7kf7vRXuyd75dJEaSzAIZ1GDWjIyRM

将接收到的数据和 oauth2 端点传递到项目中的帐户链接设置：

实现后端

为了通知 Google Home 我们的设备状态，当 Google Home 通过 Fulfillment URL 请求数据时，我们需要返回他们的数据。 同步请求的响应将返回所有设备及其功能的列表：

 # Request example
{ "requestId" : "ff36a3cc-ec34-11e6-b1a0-64510650abcf" , "inputs" : [{ "intent" : "action.devices.SYNC" }]
}

# Response example
{ "requestId" : "6894439706274654512" , "payload" : { "agentUserId" : "user123" , "devices" : [ { "id" : 1 , "type" : "action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT" , "traits" : [ "action.devices.traits.TemperatureSetting" ], "name" : { "name" : "Thermostat" }, "willReportState" : true , "attributes" : { "availableThermostatModes" : [ "off" , "heat" , "cool" ], "thermostatTemperatureRange" : { "minThresholdCelsius" : 18 , "maxThresholdCelsius" : 30 }, "thermostatTemperatureUnit" : "C" }, "deviceInfo" : { "manufacturer" : "smart-home-inc" } } ] }
}

查询请求的响应必须包含请求设备支持的每个特征的完整状态集：

 # Request example
{ "requestId" : "ff36a3cc-ec34-11e6-b1a0-64510650abcf" , "inputs" : [{ "intent" : "action.devices.QUERY" , "payload" : { "devices" : [{ "id" : "1" }] } }]
}

# Response example
{ "requestId" : "6894439706274654514" , "payload" : { "agentUserId" : "user123" , "devices" : { "1" : { "status" : "SUCCESS" , "online" : true , "thermostatMode" : "cool" , "thermostatTemperatureSetpoint" : 23 , "thermostatTemperatureAmbient" : 10 , "thermostatHumidityAmbient" : 10 } } }
}

执行请求包含与查询相同的数据，但可以包含给一组设备的命令（响应与查询相同）：

 # request example
{ "inputs" : [ { "context" : { "locale_country" : "US" , "locale_language" : "en" }, "intent" : "action.devices.EXECUTE" , "payload" : { "commands" : [ { "devices" : [ { "id" : "1" } ], "execution" : [ { "command" : "action.devices.commands.ThermostatTemperatureSetpoint" , "params" : { "thermostatTemperatureSetpoint" : 25.5 } } ] } ] } } ], "requestId" : "15039538743185198388"
}

了解设备对象

同步请求上的设备对象必须包含有关设备的信息，例如其名称、设备信息，包括特征和基于包含的火车的属性：

 # Device object on sync request
{ "id" : 1 , "type" : "action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT" , "traits" : [ "action.devices.traits.TemperatureSetting" ], "name" : { "name" : "Thermostat" }, "willReportState" : true , "attributes" : { "availableThermostatModes" : [ "off" , "heat" , "cool" ], "thermostatTemperatureRange" : { "minThresholdCelsius" : 18 , "maxThresholdCelsius" : 30 }, "thermostatTemperatureUnit" : "C" }, "deviceInfo" : { "manufacturer" : "smart-home-inc" }
}

并且必须包含 Quest 或 Execute 的设备状态：

 # Device object on Query or Execute request
{ "status" : "SUCCESS" , "online" : true , "thermostatMode" : "cool" , "thermostatTemperatureSetpoint" : 24 , "thermostatTemperatureAmbient" : 10 , "thermostatHumidityAmbient" : 10
}

返回并保存设备状态

移动到你的 Laravel 项目并创建一个 Thermostat 模型：

 php artisan make:model Thermostat -m
 # database/migrations/2022_08_11_154357_create_thermostats_table.php <?php use Illuminate\Database\Migrations\Migration ; use Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Schema ; return new class extends Migration { public function up () { Schema :: create ( 'thermostats' , function ( Blueprint $table ) { $table -> id (); $table -> boolean ( 'online' ) -> default ( false ); $table -> string ( 'mode' ); $table -> unsignedInteger ( 'current_temperature' ) -> default ( 0 ); $table -> unsignedInteger ( 'expected_temperature' ) -> default ( 15 ); $table -> unsignedInteger ( 'humidity' ) -> default ( 0 ); $table -> timestamps (); }); } public function down () { Schema :: dropIfExists ( 'thermostats' ); } };
 # app/Models/Thermostate.php <?php namespace App\Models ; use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model ; class Thermostat extends Model { protected $fillable = [ 'online' , 'mode' , 'current_temperature' , 'expected_temperature' , 'humidity' , ]; protected $casts = [ 'online' => 'boolean' , ]; }

实施 Fulfillment URL 路由

# routes/api.php <?php use App\Http\Controllers\FulfillmentController ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Route ; Route :: post ( '/' , FulfillmentController :: class );
 <?php namespace App\Http\Controllers ; use App\Models\Thermostat ; use Illuminate\Http\Request ; use Illuminate\Support\Arr ; class FulfillmentController extends Controller { public function __invoke ( Request $request ) { $response = null ; // Extract request type switch ( $request -> input ( 'inputs.0.intent' )) { case 'action.devices.QUERY' : $response = $this -> queryResponse (); break ; case 'action.devices.SYNC' : $response = $this -> syncRequest (); break ; case 'action.devices.EXECUTE' : $response = $this -> syncExecute ( $this -> syncExecute ( $request -> input ( 'inputs.0.payload.commands' ))); // Extract list of commands break ; } return $response ; } private function queryResponse () { $devices = []; // Extract our devices states foreach ( Thermostat :: all () as $thermostat ) { $devices [ $thermostat -> id ] = [ 'status' => 'SUCCESS' , 'online' => $thermostat -> online , 'thermostatMode' => $thermostat -> mode , 'thermostatTemperatureSetpoint' => $thermostat -> expected_temperature , 'thermostatTemperatureAmbient' => $thermostat -> current_temperature , 'thermostatHumidityAmbient' => $thermostat -> humidity , ]; } return response ([ 'requestId' => "6894439706274654514" , 'payload' => [ "agentUserId" => "user123" , 'devices' => $devices , ], ]); } private function syncRequest () { $devices = []; // Define our devices foreach ( Thermostat :: all () as $thermostat ) { $devices [] = [ 'id' => $thermostat -> id , 'type' => "action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT" , 'traits' => [ "action.devices.traits.TemperatureSetting" ], 'name' => [ 'name' => 'Thermostat' ], 'willReportState' => true , 'attributes' => [ 'availableThermostatModes' => [ 'off' , 'heat' , 'cool' , ], 'thermostatTemperatureRange' => [ 'minThresholdCelsius' => 18 , 'maxThresholdCelsius' => 30 , ], 'thermostatTemperatureUnit' => 'C' ], 'deviceInfo' => [ 'manufacturer' => 'smart-home-inc' , ], ]; } return response ([ 'requestId' => "6894439706274654512" , 'payload' => [ "agentUserId" => "user123" , 'devices' => $devices , ], ]); } private function syncExecute ( array $commands ) { foreach ( $commands as $command ) { // Get devices for execute command $thermostats = Thermostat :: whereIn ( 'id' , Arr :: pluck ( $command [ 'devices' ], 'id' )) -> get (); foreach ( $command [ 'execution' ] as $executionItem ) { switch ( $executionItem [ 'command' ]) { // Handle set point command and save it in our model case 'action.devices.commands.ThermostatTemperatureSetpoint' : foreach ( $thermostats as $thermostat ) { $thermostat -> update ([ 'expected_temperature' => $executionItem [ 'params' ][ 'thermostatTemperatureSetpoint' ], ]); } break ; // Handle set set mode command and save it in our model case 'action.devices.commands.ThermostatSetMode' : foreach ( $thermostats as $thermostat ) { $thermostat -> update ([ 'mode' => $executionItem [ 'params' ][ 'thermostatMode' ], ]); } break ; } } } // It not necessary to return data for command request return response ([]); } }

返回控制台操作并在项目设置中设置您的 Fulfillment URL：
并在您的数据库中创建您的设备：

简化认证过程：

由于我们对认证细节不感兴趣，我们可以跳过实现登录页面并强制认证用户：

 # app/Providers/AppServiceProvider.php <?php namespace App\Providers ; use App\Models\User ; use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Auth ; use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider ; class AppServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider { public function register () { // } public function boot () { Auth :: setUser ( User :: first ()); } }

将后端与 Google Home 关联

在之前的操作之后，您可以使用 Google Home 应用程序测试您的后端，该应用程序经过身份验证，与用于创建操作控制台项目的帐户相同。
关联后，您的设备将出现在 Google Home 中：


现在您可以控制您的“设备”，其状态将保存在数据库中。 您可以触发同步（重新连接帐户）、查询（滑动更新手势）和执行（尝试更改模式）。

您可以在此处找到有关可用设备类型和特征的更多信息

技术解决方案 - 一旦暂停，davidnadejdin 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，davidnadejdin 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，davidnadejdin 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 davidnadejdin 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 davidnadejdin 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 davidnadejdin 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

We will use Cloud-to-cloud integration method. So we need to navigate to Actions on Google console and create a new project:


And we need to name our Smart Home Action:


Set up OAuth2 and backend server

Cloud-to-cloud integration required OAuth2 server for linking your smart device service to Google Home application. In this article, we will implement it with Laravel framework and Passport package. You should pass base steps, like Laravel and Passport installation, database setup, and make fake users or you can find example code here. All additional actions will be described next.

Account linking

Move to your Laravel project and run the command to generate OAuth Client Information for Google Home.

$ php artisan passport:client Which user ID should the client be assigned to?:
> 1 What should we name the client?:
> Google Where should we redirect the request after authorization?:
> https://oauth-redirect.googleusercontent.com/r/{your project id}

New client created successfully.
Client ID: 9700039b-92b7-4a79-a421-152747b9a257
Client secret: 813PEwdTAq7kf7vRXuyd75dJEaSzAIZ1GDWjIyRM

Pass received data and oauth2 endpoints to Account Linking settings in your project:

Implement backend

To notify Google Home about our device status, we need to return their data when Google Home requested data by Fulfillment URL. Google Home sends data in 3 types: SYNC, QUERY, and EXECUTE.

Response for sync request will return a list of all devices and their capabilities:

# Request example
{ "requestId": "ff36a3cc-ec34-11e6-b1a0-64510650abcf", "inputs": [{ "intent": "action.devices.SYNC" }]
}

# Response example
{ "requestId": "6894439706274654512", "payload": { "agentUserId": "user123", "devices": [ { "id": 1, "type": "action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT", "traits": [ "action.devices.traits.TemperatureSetting" ], "name": { "name": "Thermostat" }, "willReportState": true, "attributes": { "availableThermostatModes": [ "off", "heat", "cool" ], "thermostatTemperatureRange": { "minThresholdCelsius": 18, "maxThresholdCelsius": 30 }, "thermostatTemperatureUnit": "C" }, "deviceInfo": { "manufacturer": "smart-home-inc" } } ] }
}

Resposne for query request must include a full set of states for each of the traits supported by the requested devices:

# Request example
{ "requestId": "ff36a3cc-ec34-11e6-b1a0-64510650abcf", "inputs": [{ "intent": "action.devices.QUERY", "payload": { "devices": [{ "id": "1" }] } }]
}

# Response example
{ "requestId": "6894439706274654514", "payload": { "agentUserId": "user123", "devices": { "1": { "status": "SUCCESS", "online": true, "thermostatMode": "cool", "thermostatTemperatureSetpoint": 23, "thermostatTemperatureAmbient": 10, "thermostatHumidityAmbient": 10 } } }
}

Execute request contain data as same as Query but can contain commands given to a group of devices(response is same as Query):

# request example
{ "inputs": [ { "context": { "locale_country": "US", "locale_language": "en" }, "intent": "action.devices.EXECUTE", "payload": { "commands": [ { "devices": [ { "id": "1" } ], "execution": [ { "command": "action.devices.commands.ThermostatTemperatureSetpoint", "params": { "thermostatTemperatureSetpoint": 25.5 } } ] } ] } } ], "requestId": "15039538743185198388"
}

Understanding device object

A device object on Sync request must contain information about a device like its name, device info, including traits, and attributes based on included trains:

# Device object on sync request
{ "id": 1, "type": "action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT", "traits": [ "action.devices.traits.TemperatureSetting" ], "name": { "name": "Thermostat" }, "willReportState": true, "attributes": { "availableThermostatModes": [ "off", "heat", "cool" ], "thermostatTemperatureRange": { "minThresholdCelsius": 18, "maxThresholdCelsius": 30 }, "thermostatTemperatureUnit": "C" }, "deviceInfo": { "manufacturer": "smart-home-inc" }
}

And must contain a device state on Quest or Execute:

# Device object on Query or Execute request
{ "status": "SUCCESS", "online": true, "thermostatMode": "cool", "thermostatTemperatureSetpoint": 24, "thermostatTemperatureAmbient": 10, "thermostatHumidityAmbient": 10
}

Return and save device state

Move to your Laravel project and create a Thermostat model:

php artisan make:model Thermostat -m

# database/migrations/2022_08_11_154357_create_thermostats_table.php <?php use Illuminate\Database\Migrations\Migration;
use Illuminate\Database\Schema\Blueprint;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Schema; return new class extends Migration
{ public function up() { Schema::create('thermostats', function (Blueprint $table) { $table->id(); $table->boolean('online')->default(false); $table->string('mode'); $table->unsignedInteger('current_temperature')->default(0); $table->unsignedInteger('expected_temperature')->default(15); $table->unsignedInteger('humidity')->default(0); $table->timestamps(); }); } public function down() { Schema::dropIfExists('thermostats'); }
};

# app/Models/Thermostate.php
<?php namespace App\Models; use Illuminate\Database\Eloquent\Model; class Thermostat extends Model
{ protected $fillable = [ 'online', 'mode', 'current_temperature', 'expected_temperature', 'humidity', ]; protected $casts = [ 'online' => 'boolean', ];
}

Implement Fulfillment URL route 

# routes/api.php <?php use App\Http\Controllers\FulfillmentController;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Route; Route::post('/', FulfillmentController::class);

<?php namespace App\Http\Controllers; use App\Models\Thermostat;
use Illuminate\Http\Request;
use Illuminate\Support\Arr; class FulfillmentController extends Controller
{ public function __invoke(Request $request) { $response = null; // Extract request type switch ($request->input('inputs.0.intent')) { case 'action.devices.QUERY': $response = $this->queryResponse(); break; case 'action.devices.SYNC': $response = $this->syncRequest(); break; case 'action.devices.EXECUTE': $response = $this->syncExecute($this->syncExecute($request->input('inputs.0.payload.commands'))); // Extract list of commands break; } return $response; } private function queryResponse() { $devices = []; // Extract our devices states foreach (Thermostat::all() as $thermostat) { $devices[$thermostat->id] = [ 'status' => 'SUCCESS', 'online' => $thermostat->online, 'thermostatMode' => $thermostat->mode, 'thermostatTemperatureSetpoint' => $thermostat->expected_temperature, 'thermostatTemperatureAmbient' => $thermostat->current_temperature, 'thermostatHumidityAmbient' => $thermostat->humidity, ]; } return response([ 'requestId' => "6894439706274654514", 'payload' => [ "agentUserId" => "user123", 'devices' => $devices, ], ]); } private function syncRequest() { $devices = []; // Define our devices foreach (Thermostat::all() as $thermostat) { $devices[] = [ 'id' => $thermostat->id, 'type' => "action.devices.types.THERMOSTAT", 'traits' => [ "action.devices.traits.TemperatureSetting" ], 'name' => [ 'name' => 'Thermostat' ], 'willReportState' => true, 'attributes' => [ 'availableThermostatModes' => [ 'off', 'heat', 'cool', ], 'thermostatTemperatureRange' => [ 'minThresholdCelsius' => 18, 'maxThresholdCelsius' => 30, ], 'thermostatTemperatureUnit' => 'C' ], 'deviceInfo' => [ 'manufacturer' => 'smart-home-inc', ], ]; } return response([ 'requestId' => "6894439706274654512", 'payload' => [ "agentUserId" => "user123", 'devices' => $devices, ], ]); } private function syncExecute(array $commands) { foreach ($commands as $command) { // Get devices for execute command $thermostats = Thermostat::whereIn('id', Arr::pluck($command['devices'], 'id'))->get(); foreach ($command['execution'] as $executionItem) { switch ($executionItem['command']) { // Handle set point command and save it in our model case 'action.devices.commands.ThermostatTemperatureSetpoint': foreach ($thermostats as $thermostat) { $thermostat->update([ 'expected_temperature' => $executionItem['params']['thermostatTemperatureSetpoint'], ]); } break; // Handle set set mode command and save it in our model case 'action.devices.commands.ThermostatSetMode': foreach ($thermostats as $thermostat) { $thermostat->update([ 'mode' => $executionItem['params']['thermostatMode'], ]); } break; } } } // It not necessary to return data for command request return response([]); }
}

Return to Console actions and set your Fulfillment URL in your project settings:
And create your device in your database:

Simplify the authentication process:

Since we are not interested in authentication details we can skip the implement login page and force the authenticated user:

# app/Providers/AppServiceProvider.php <?php namespace App\Providers; use App\Models\User;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Auth;
use Illuminate\Support\ServiceProvider; class AppServiceProvider extends ServiceProvider
{ public function register() { // } public function boot() { Auth::setUser(User::first()); }
}

Link backend with Google Home

After previous actions, you can test your backend with Google Home app which is authenticated as the same account used for creating Actions Console project.
After linking your device will appear in Google Home:


Now you can control your "device" and its state will be saved on the database. You can trigger sync(reconnect account), query(swipe update gesture), and execute(try to change mode).

You can find more about available device types and traits Here

Techsolutionstuff - Jun 20
