item.title: 开发商股票期权初学者指南 | A beginners guide to developer stock options
item.description:
你和我的位置一样吗？ 您是否听说过开发商股票期权，但不知道术语的含义？ 不要再看了！ 在这份开发商股票期权初学者指南中，我们将了解开发商股票期权的工作原理，以及您应该了解哪些术语！

老实说，我从来没有将开发商股票期权作为我总薪酬的一部分。 我认为这是为美国初创公司、C 级高管或敢于在初创公司的非常早期阶段实现飞跃的人保留的。 但事实证明，越来越多的公司正在发放开发商股票期权。 如果您正在寻找新工作，请继续阅读，您会在下一次薪酬谈判中了解开发商的股票期权。

什么是开发商股票期权？

开发人员股票期权是一种在默认工资之外获得额外报酬的方法。 您可以选择以股票的形式购买公司的一部分。 而且通常以比当前市场价值更便宜的价格。

但是，开发商股票期权绝不是保证。 公司可能不会上市，或者股票的市场价值可能远低于预期。 与所有财务问题一样，请在做出任何决定之前进行适当的研究。

必须知道条款

要了解开发商股票期权，最好了解所有术语和缩写。

股票期权是本文的重点。 股票期权赋予您在固定期限内以固定价格购买一定数量股票的权利（但非义务）。

悬崖

您通常无法立即购买您的开发商股票期权。 您购买期权所需的时间称为悬崖。 在您购买期权之前，公司通常会规定您需要受雇的最短期限。 例如，您需要在公司至少呆 1 年才能购买股票。

归属

归属是您获得开发商股票期权的部分。 假设您在 5 年内获得 100 个选项。 您可以一年获得 20 个，一个季度获得 5 个，等等。

克里夫也参与了归属。 如果你有 1 年的悬崖期，你通常会在一年后获得第一年的期权，然后按照归属时间表进行。

行使价

行使价是您可以购买股票的预定价格。

如果您的行使价为 40 美元，而您股票的市场价值为 50 美元，那么您在卖出时将获得 10 美元。 不要忘记也考虑您当地的税法。

您获得的不是购买股票的选择权，而是实际的股票。

例如，您可以获得 100 只股票或价值 10,000 美元的股票。

员工持股计划

您可以在其中找到您的开发商股票期权计划的条款和条件。

结论

我希望这篇博文能帮助您了解开发者股票期权的基础知识。 我们讨论了开发商股票期权、悬崖、归属、行使价和其他几个主题。 如果你想深入了解，我推荐 Gergely Orosz的软件工程师 Equity 101和伟大的书Rewarding Talent 。

如果您喜欢这篇文章，我建议您阅读开发人员基础系列中的数学在编程中的重要性。



原文:

Are you in the same position as me? Do you hear about developer stock options, but have no idea what the terminology means? Look no further! In this beginner's guide to developer stock options, we will take a look at how developer stock options work, and what terms you should know!

Honestly, I have never had developer stock options as part of my total compensation package. I thought this was reserved for American startups, C-level executives, or people that dare to take the leap at a very-very early stage at a startup. But it turns out more and more companies are handing out developer stock options. If you are looking for a new job, read along and you understand developer stock options in your next compensation negotiation.

What are developer stock options?

Developer stock options are a way to get an extra level of compensation on top of your default salary. You get the option to buy a piece of the company in form of stocks. And usually at a cheaper rate than the current market value.

However, developer stock options are never a guarantee. The company might not go public, or the market value of the shares might be much lower than anticipated. As with all financial matters, please do proper research before you make any decisions.

Must know terms

To understand developer stock options, it is a good idea to understand all terminology and abbreviations.

(Stock) Options

Stock options are the main point of this article here. A stock option gives you the right (but not obligation) to buy a given amount of shares at a set price for a fixed period.

Cliff

You normally will not be able to buy your developer stock options right away. The period it takes for you to be able to buy your options is called Cliff. Companies usually apply a minimum period you need to be employed before you are able to buy your options. For example, you need to stay at the company for a minimum of 1 year before you are able to buy your shares.

Vesting

Vesting is in what portions you get your developer stock options. Let's say you get 100 options over 5 years. You can get 20 a year, 5 a quarter, etc.

Cliff comes to play in vesting as well. If you have a cliff period of 1 year, you will usually get your first year of options after a year, and then follow the vesting schedule.

Strike price

The strike price is the predefined price at which you can buy the shares.

If you have a strike price of $40 and the market value of your share is $50, you will gain $10 when you sell. Don't forget to take your local tax laws into account as well.

RSU

RSU's or restricted stock units are an alternative to stock options. Instead of getting the option to buy stocks, you get the actual stocks.

RSU's usually have 2 forms, either you get a predefined amount of shares, or you get a number of shares based on a predefined amount in value. For example, you can get 100 stocks or $10,000 worth of stocks.

ESOP

ESOP or employee stock option plan is the legal framework that is used to give options to employees. You can find the terms and conditions of your developer stock option plan in there.

Conclusion

I hope this blog post helped you to understand the basics of developer stock options. We discussed developer stock options, cliff, vesting, strike price, and several more topics. If you want to dive deeper, I recommend Equity 101 for software engineers by Gergely Orosz, and the great book Rewarding talent.

In case you liked this post, I can recommend reading the How important is math in programming post in the developer basics series.

item.title: 编码和编程有什么区别？ | What Is the Difference Between Coding and Programming?
item.description:
编码和编程有什么区别？
作为一名寻求技术工作的新 Web 开发人员，您应该了解编码和编程之间的特殊区别。

编码与计算机的语法有关，而编程与程序的思维过程有关。

为什么这种区别很重要？ 因为雇主雇用程序员！

在本文中，我们将介绍编码和编程之间的区别，以及为什么雇主雇用程序员而不是编码员！

什么是编码？

编码与计算机语言的语法有关。

好的，那么“语法”到底是什么？ 维基百科将语法定义为“定义符号组合的一组规则，这些符号组合被认为是该语言中结构正确的语句或表达式。”

简单来说，编码是“说”计算机语言的过程。 就像口语一样，有许多计算机语言。 将计算机语法视为与使用问号和感叹号的标点规则有关的英语语言的一部分。 当您从英语翻译成西班牙语时，这些规则就会改变！

开发人员的语言示例包括 JavaScript、Python 和 C#。

什么是编程？

编程正在为计算机提供正确而强大的指令。

例如，在实现一个**register **form时，程序员会思考过程中的具体步骤：

  1. 验证电子邮件文本框不为空
  2. 验证电子邮件在数据库中不存在
  3. 将电子邮件异步发送到服务器
  4. 阅读响应并确保用户注册成功
  5. 如果是，则呈现下一页否则，从响应中读取错误消息并向用户显示人性化的版本
  6. 只有获得指令后，才能使用代码将它们表达到计算机上。

可以说，懂代码就像懂英语，懂编程就是懂写论文。

对英语语法的深入理解并不一定会让你成为一个优秀的讲故事的人。 同样，许多新开发人员专注于掌握一门编码语言，但这不会让你成为一名出色的程序员。

现代开发人员既是编码员又是程序员

在今天更强大的编程语言之前，过去，程序是由人们用复杂的机器语言编写的。

维基百科解释说， “从计算机编程语言的发明到 1970 年代中期，大多数计算机程序员在穿孔卡片上逐行创建、编辑和存储他们的程序。”

摄影：Kartenlocher - pixabay.com

以这种方式编写计算机指令容易出错且耗时。 程序员的时间不被认为是“值得”的，因为他们应该专注于思考强大的解决方案。 程序员被雇佣来接受程序员出色的指令，并让计算机执行它们。

从那时起，计算世界已经走过了漫长的道路。

随着编程语言年复一年地发展，它使编写程序更加高效且不易出错。 程序员不再需要委派编码任务，因为程序员已经承担了这两个角色！

虽然演奏音乐需要专业技能，但作曲家使用复杂的音乐理论创作了这部作品。 当心教程地狱🔥

许多新的 Web 开发人员犯的一个常见错误是只通过观看“掌握 JavaScript”课程或从头到尾阅读 JavaScript 教科书来专注于编码。

这感觉很有成效，但如果您只遵循教程，您可能很难在没有它们的情况下构建应用程序。

那么如何摆脱这种非生产性的循环呢？ 通过编写自己的程序！

当您编写自己的程序时，您将面临通过反复试验以及大量研究来解决问题，从而使您成为更好的程序员！ 学习如何编码和编程需要多长时间？

给自己时间去学习！

你可能会在 21 天内遇到 Learn C++等内容，相信你可以在一个月内学会这门语言。 尽管您可以在该时间范围内学习语法，但开发编程技能需要更长的时间。

你必须给自己时间来正确地学习。 按照自己指定的时间表匆忙完成课程对你没有任何好处。 您可能会按时完成，但可能无法保留您正在学习的内容。 您最终可能会感到沮丧，甚至可能完全放弃学习如何编码！ 每个人都按照自己的节奏以不同的方式学习。 不要将您的学习时间表与他人比较！

不过，您一定想知道：

要学习最低限度并达到成为可雇用的前端开发人员的水平，预计需要6-12 个月。

请记住，尽管您可以在很短的自定时间表内学习编码，但学习如何编程是您将在整个职业生涯中不断获得的技能。

招聘经理正在寻找什么

公司正在寻求雇用程序员，而不是编码员。

在白板面试期间，您将被评估为有效解决和解决问题的能力。

有些面试甚至与编码语言无关，允许您选择语言。 这是因为面试官认为任何聪明的程序员都可以在短时间内学习任何编码语言。

所以你的目标是成为一个高效的程序员，而不是任何特定语言的专家。 作为一名高效的程序员，您可以学习任何语言的语法，然后利用它们。

将语言和框架视为工具包中的工具； 作为一名高效的程序员，您可以为任何给定的工作选择正确的工具！ 你有这个！

知道是成功的一半！ 了解招聘经理的需求将为您在就业市场上带来竞争优势，并为您提供成功所需的信心！

一旦您的技能集有效地达到编码和编程水平，您将能够跟上您的同事，他们甚至不会意识到您实际上已经开发了多长时间！ 判决

编码员和程序员曾经是两个不同的工作。 在当今的劳动力中，开发人员同时担任编码员和程序员的角色。

如果您不确定要学习什么语言，JavaScript 是前端和后端开发中使用的一种高需求语言。 您还可以使用 JavaScript 在 Microsoft 和 Google 应用程序中编程！

搜索本地工作和您感兴趣的其他人，以查看他们使用的框架。 尽管 React 很受欢迎，但另一个框架可能是您更好的选择。

要提高您的编码和编程技能，请不断创建您热爱的项目，并确保给自己适当的学习时间。 获得这两种技能是一场马拉松，而不是短跑！

你的目标是学习如何像程序员一样思考。 虽然有效编码的技能很重要，但招聘经理正在寻找能够通过他们的编程能力有效解决和解决问题的开发人员！

让我们连接！ 我在LinkedIn和Twitter 上很活跃。


原文:

What Is the Difference Between Coding and Programming?
As a new web developer seeking employment in tech, you should be aware of particular distinctions between coding and programming.

Coding has to do with the computer's syntax, whereas programming has to do with the thought process of the program.

Why is this distinction important? Because employers hire programmers!

In this article, we cover the difference between coding and programming and why employers hire programmers, not coders!

What is coding?

Coding has to do with the computer language's syntax.

Ok, so what exactly is “syntax?” 🤔

Wikipedia defines syntax as “the set of rules that defines the combinations of symbols that are considered to be correctly structured statements or expressions in that language.”

Keywords such as let and function, the = sign, quotes, and bracket symbols all represent the computer language's syntax.

In simpler terms, coding is the process of "speaking" the computer's language. Just like spoken languages, there are many computer languages. 💻

Think of the computer syntax as the part of the English language having to do with punctuation rules using question marks and exclamation points. When you translate from English to Spanish, these rules change!

Examples of languages for developers include JavaScript, Python, and C#.

What is programming?

Programming is coming up with correct and robust instructions for the computer.

For example, when implementing a **register **form, a programmer will think through the specific steps in the process:

  1. Validate the email text box is not empty
  2. Verify the email does not already exist in the database
  3. Send the email to the server asynchronously
  4. Read the response and ensure the user was registered successfully
  5. If so, render the next page Otherwise, read the error message from the response and show a human-friendly version to the user
  6. Only once you have the instructions can you express them to the computer using code.

It could be said that knowing code is like knowing English but knowing how to program is knowing how to write an essay.

Advanced understanding of English grammar doesn't make you a great storyteller necessarily. Similarly, many new developers focus on mastering a coding language, but that won't make you a great programmer.

Modern-day developers are both coders and programmers

Before today's more robust programming languages, in the past, programs were coded by people in complex machine languages.

Wikipedia explains, “From the invention of computer programming languages to the mid-1970s, most computer programmers created, edited, and stored their programs line by line on punch cards.”

Photo by: Kartenlocher - pixabay.com

Writing computer instructions in this way was error-prone and time-consuming. It wasn't considered "worth" the programmer's time because they should focus on thinking through robust solutions. Coders were hired to take the brilliant instructions by programmers and make the computer execute them.

The world of computing has come a long way since then.

As programming languages evolve year to year, it makes writing programs more efficient and less error-prone. The programmer no longer has to delegate the task of coding, as the programmer has taken on both roles!

🎻 Think of a coder as a musician just handed a piece of music to play written by a composer. Although it takes specialized skills to perform the music, the composer wrote the work using complex music theory. 🎼

Beware tutorial hell 🔥

One common mistake many new web developers make is focusing only on coding by watching "master JavaScript" courses or reading JavaScript textbooks cover to cover.

This feels productive but if you only follow tutorials you might struggle to build apps without them.

"Tutorial hell is when you're only able to follow other tutorials and you're stuck because you don't have a purpose, you're just "learning" to "learn". If you want to learn programming, you gotta start building your own projects."
- insertAlias on Reddit

So how do you break out of this unproductive cycle? By writing your own programs!

When you write your own programs, you are faced with resolving problems through trial and error along with plenty of research, resulting in YOU becoming a better programmer! ☝🏻

How long will it take to learn how to code and program?

Give yourself the time you need to learn!

You may come across content such as Learn C++ in 21 days, believing you can learn the language within a month. Although you may learn the syntax within that time frame, developing programming skills takes much longer.

You must give yourself the time to properly learn. It will not do you any good to rush through courses at a self-appointed timetable. You may finish on schedule but may not retain what you're learning. You could end up frustrated and might even quit learning how to code altogether! 😲

Everyone learns differently and at their own pace. Do not compare your learning timetable to others!

Still, you must be wondering:

“Yeah, but how long will it take to learn the minimum and reach the level of being a hireable frontend developer?”
To learn the minimum and reach the level of becoming a hireable frontend developer, expect it to take 6-12 months.

Remember, although you could learn coding in a short self-appointed timetable, learning how to program is a continually growing skill you will acquire throughout your career.

What hiring managers are looking for

Companies are looking to hire programmers, not coders.

During whiteboard interviews, you are being evaluated for your ability to solve and work through problems efficiently.

Some interviews are even coding language agnostic, allowing you to choose the language. This is because the interviewers believe any smart programmer can learn any coding language in a short time.

So your aim is to become an efficient programmer, not an expert in any given language. As an efficient programmer, you can learn the syntax of any language and then utilize them.

Think of languages and frameworks as tools in a tool kit; as an efficient programmer, you can pick the right tool for any given job! 🛠

You got this!

Knowing is half the battle! 💥

Understanding what hiring managers are looking for will give you a competitive advantage in the job market and provide you with the confidence you need to succeed!

Once your skillset reaches a level of coding and programming efficiently, you will be able to keep up with your colleagues, and they won't even realize how long you've actually been developing for! 😉

The verdict

Coders and programmers were once two different jobs. In today's workforce, a developer takes on the role of both coder and programmer.

If you are unsure what language to learn, JavaScript is a high in demand language used in Frontend and Backend development. You can also use JavaScript to program in Microsoft and Google applications!

Search local jobs and others you are interested in to see what Frameworks they use. Although React is popular, another Framework might be a better option for you.

To improve your coding and programming skills, continuously create projects you are passionate about, and make sure you give yourself the time you need to properly learn. Acquiring both skills is a marathon, not a sprint!

Your goal is to learn how to think like a programmer. While the skill of coding efficiently is important, hiring managers are seeking developers with the ability to solve and work through problems efficiently through their programming ability!

Let's connect! I'm active on LinkedIn and Twitter.
item.author: michaellarocca

item.title: Angular 中的动态翻译成为可能 | Dynamic translations in Angular made possible
item.description:
迈克尔·M。

发表于如果您曾经在 Angular 中处理过国际化（或简称为“i18n”）或即将实现它，您可能会坚持使用很棒的官方指南，使用可能难以调试的第三方包或选择替代方案我将在下面描述的路径。

使用 i18n 时的常见缺陷之一是翻译文件很大并且无法拆分它们以隐藏应用程序的某些部分以防窥探。 像 Angular 内置实现这样的解决方案非常强大并且与 SEO 兼容，但需要大量准备工作，并且不支持在开发模式下动态切换语言（至少在版本 9 中造成了麻烦）； 虽然对于支持一切并适合所有人的复杂功能没有“魔杖”，但这里有另一种实现翻译的方法可能适合您的需求。
介绍够了，我保证这将是一个实用指南，所以让我们直接进入它。

准备基础

第一步是为将在整个应用程序中使用的语言创建一个类型：

 export type LanguageCode = 'en' | 'de';

最受欢迎的 Angular 功能之一是依赖注入，它为我们做了很多工作——让我们利用它来满足我们的需求。 我还想通过在本指南中使用 NgRx 来增加一些趣味，但如果您不在项目中使用它，请随意用简单的 BehaviorSubject 替换它。

作为使 NgRx 的进一步开发更容易的可选步骤，为 DI 工厂创建一个类型：

 export type Ti18nFactory<Part> = (store: Store) => Observable<Part>;

创建翻译文件

通用字符串

假设我们有一些我们想在整个应用程序中使用的基本字符串。 一些从不与特定模块、功能或库相关的简单但常见的东西，例如“确定”或“返回”按钮。 我们将把这些字符串放在“核心”模块中，并从一个简单的界面开始这样做，这将帮助我们不要忘记输出翻译中的任何单个字符串： 

 export interface I18nCore { errorDefault: string; language: string; }

需要明确的是，这个接口并不能保证所有字符串都会被实际翻译，但是如果你忘记在“lang”文件中包含任何字符串，TypeScript 编译器（和你的 IDE）会引发错误“TS2741”。

继续实现接口和此代码段，我提供一个示例文件路径非常重要，在这种情况下为libs/core/src/lib/i18n/lang-en.lang.ts ：

 export const lang: I18nCore = { errorDefault: 'An error has occurred', language: 'Language', };

为了减少代码重复并充分利用开发过程，我们还将创建一个 DI 工厂。 这是一个使用 NgRx 的工作示例（同样，这是完全可选的，您可以为此使用 BehaviorSubject）：

 export const I18N_CORE = new InjectionToken<Observable<I18nCore>>('I18N_CORE'); export const i18nCoreFactory: Ti18nFactory<I18nCore> = (store: Store): Observable<I18nCore> => (store as Store<LocalePartialState>).pipe( select(getLocaleLanguageCode), distinctUntilChanged(), switchMap((code: LanguageCode) => import(`./lang-${code}.lang`) .then((l: { lang: I18nCore }) => l.lang) ), ); export const i18nCoreProvider: FactoryProvider = { provide: I18N_CORE, useFactory: i18nCoreFactory, deps: [Store], };

显然， getLocaleLanguageCode选择器将从 Store 中选择语言代码。

不要忘记将翻译文件包含在您的编译中，因为它们不会被直接引用，因此不会自动包含在内。 为此，找到相关的“tsconfig”（列出“main.ts”的那个）并将以下内容添加到“include”数组中：

 "../../libs/core/src/lib/i18n/*.lang.ts"

请注意，此处的文件路径包含一个通配符，以便您的所有翻译将立即包含在内。 另外，出于个人喜好，我喜欢为类似的文件添加前缀，这几乎可以解释为什么示例名称（ [prefix]-[langCode]**.lang.ts** ）看起来如此奇怪。

特定于模块的字符串

让我们对任何模块做同样的事情，这样我们就可以看到翻译将如何在浏览器中单独加载。 为了简单起见，这个模块将被命名为“tab1”。

再次，从界面开始：

 export interface I18nTab1 { country: string; }

实现这个接口：

 export const lang: I18nTab1 = { country: 'Country', };

将您的翻译包含在编译中：

 "../../libs/tab1/src/lib/i18n/*.lang.ts"

并且可以选择创建一个 DI 工厂，该工厂看起来与以前的完全相同，但具有另一个接口。

提供翻译

我更喜欢减少提供程序的数量，因此“核心”翻译将仅在AppModule中列出：

 providers: [i18nCoreProvider],

任何其他翻译都应该只在相关模块中提供 - 在延迟加载的功能模块中，或者如果您遵循 SCAM 模式，在组件模块中：

 @NgModule({ declarations: [TabComponent], imports: [CommonModule, ReactiveFormsModule], providers: [i18nTab1Provider], }) export class TabModule {}

还要注意使用预制 FactoryProviders 而不是在此处添加对象的优雅。

在component.ts中注入令牌：

 constructor( @Inject(I18N_CORE) public readonly i18nCore$: Observable<I18nCore>, @Inject(I18N_TAB1) public readonly i18nTab1$: Observable<I18nTab1>, ) {}

最后，用 ng-container 和一个简单的 ngIf 语句包装component.html ：

 <ng-container *ngIf="{ core: i18nCore$ | async, tab1: i18nTab1$ | async } as i18n"> <p>{{ i18n.core?.language }}</p> <p>{{ i18n.tab1?.country }}: n/a</p> </ng-container>

检查结果

让我们运行它，看看它是否真的有效，更重要的是如何准确地加载这些翻译。 我创建了一个简单的演示应用程序，其中包含两个延迟加载的 Angular 模块，因此您可以克隆和试验它。 但现在，这里是 DevTools 的实际截图：

这是开发模式下的初始页面加载； 请注意最后的两个.js文件——我们在上一节中创建了这些文件。

这是切换语言时的样子。 出于演示目的，“网络”选项卡已重置。

这是切换到第二个惰性选项卡的结果。

好处

  • 使用此解决方案，您可以但没有义务以您需要的任何方式将您的翻译拆分为多个文件；
  • 它是反应式的，这意味着正确实施它可以为您的用户提供无缝体验；
  • 它不需要您安装任何开箱即用的 Angular 不附带的东西；
  • 它易于调试且完全可定制，因为它将直接在您的项目中实现；
  • 它支持复杂的区域设置解决方案，例如与浏览器语言相关联、在授权后从用户帐户中获取区域设置以及使用用户定义的语言覆盖——所有这些都无需重新加载单个页面；
  • 它还支持现代 IDE 中的代码完成。

缺点

  • 由于这些翻译文件不会包含在资产中，它们实际上应该被转译，这会稍微增加构建时间；
  • 它要求您创建自定义实用程序或使用第三方解决方案与本地化平台交换您的翻译；
  • 如果没有适当的服务器端渲染，它可能无法很好地与搜索引擎一起使用。

随意尝试此存储库中提供的完整工作示例。
保持积极态度并创建出色的应用程序！

波格丹·亚历山德鲁·米利塔鲁 - 蒂亚戈-席尔瓦-洛佩斯一旦暂停，gin_mitch 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，gin_mitch 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，所有 gin_mitch 的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 gin_mitch 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 gin_mitch 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 gin_mitch 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Michael M.

Posted on Aug 12

If you have ever dealt with internationalization (or “i18n” for short) in Angular or is about to implement it, you may stick with the official guide which is awesome, use third-party packages that might be hard to debug or choose an alternative path which I will describe below.

One of the common pitfalls when using i18n are large translation files size and inability to split them in order to hide parts of your application from prying eyes. Some solutions like Angular built-in implementation are really powerful and SEO compatible but require a lot of preparation and do not support switching languages on the fly in development mode (which was causing troubles at least in version 9); other solutions like ngx-translate require you to install several packages and still don’t support splitting up a single language.

While there is no “magic wand” out there for this complex feature that supports everything and fits everyone, here is another way of implementing translations that might fit your needs.
Enough with the introduction, I promised this would be a practical guide, so let’s jump straight into it.

Preparing the basics

The first step is to create a type for languages that will be used across the app:

export type LanguageCode = 'en' | 'de';

One of the loved Angular features is Dependency Injection that does a lot for us — let’s utilize it for our needs. I would also like to spice things up a little by using NgRx for this guide but if you don’t use it in your project, feel free to replace it with a simple BehaviorSubject.

As an optional step that will make further development with NgRx easier, create a type for DI factories:

export type Ti18nFactory<Part> = (store: Store) => Observable<Part>;

Creating translation files

General strings

Suppose we have some basic strings that we’d like to use across the app. Some simple yet common things that are never related to a specific module, feature or library, like “OK” or “Back” buttons. We will place these strings in “core” module and start doing so with a simple interface that will help us not to forget any single string in out translations:

export interface I18nCore {
  errorDefault: string;
  language: string;
}

Just to be clear, this interface does not guarantee that all the strings will be actually translated but the TypeScript compiler (and your IDE) will raise an error “TS2741” if you forget to include any string in your “lang” files.

Moving on to the implementation for the interface and for this snippet it’s vitally important that I provide an example file path which in this case would be libs/core/src/lib/i18n/lang-en.lang.ts:

export const lang: I18nCore = {
  errorDefault: 'An error has occurred',
  language: 'Language',
};

To reduce code duplication and get the most out of the development process, we’ll also create a DI factory. Here’s a working example utilizing NgRx (again, this is completely optional, you may use BehaviorSubject for this):

export const I18N_CORE =
  new InjectionToken<Observable<I18nCore>>('I18N_CORE');

export const i18nCoreFactory: Ti18nFactory<I18nCore> =
  (store: Store): Observable<I18nCore> => 
    (store as Store<LocalePartialState>).pipe(
      select(getLocaleLanguageCode),
      distinctUntilChanged(),
      switchMap((code: LanguageCode) =>
        import(`./lang-${code}.lang`)
          .then((l: { lang: I18nCore }) => l.lang)
      ),
    );

export const i18nCoreProvider: FactoryProvider = {
  provide: I18N_CORE,
  useFactory: i18nCoreFactory,
  deps: [Store],
};

Obviously, the getLocaleLanguageCode selector will pick the language code from Store.

Don’t forget to include translation files into your compilation as they are not being referenced directly thus will not be automatically included. For that, locate the relevant “tsconfig” (the one that lists “main.ts”) and add the following to the “include” array:

"../../libs/core/src/lib/i18n/*.lang.ts"

Note that the file path here includes a wildcard so that all your translations will be included at once. Also, as a matter of taste, I like to prefix similar files which pretty much explains why the example name ([prefix]-[langCode]**.lang.ts**) looks so weird.

Module-specific strings

Let’s do the same for any module, so we can see how translations will be loaded separately in the browser. To keep it simple, this module would be named “tab1”.

Again, start with the interface:

export interface I18nTab1 {
  country: string;
}

Implement this interface:

export const lang: I18nTab1 = {
  country: 'Country',
};

Include your translations into compilation:

"../../libs/tab1/src/lib/i18n/*.lang.ts"

And optionally create a DI factory which would look literally the same as previous but with another interface.

Providing translations

I prefer to reduce the amount of providers so “core” translations will be listed in AppModule only:

providers: [i18nCoreProvider],

Any other translation should be provided in the relevant modules only — either in lazy-loaded feature modules or, if you follow the SCAM pattern, in component modules:

@NgModule({
  declarations: [TabComponent],
  imports: [CommonModule, ReactiveFormsModule],
  providers: [i18nTab1Provider],
})
export class TabModule {}

Also note the elegance of utilizing pre-made FactoryProviders instead of adding objects here.

Inject the tokens in a component.ts:

constructor(
  @Inject(I18N_CORE)
  public readonly i18nCore$: Observable<I18nCore>,
  @Inject(I18N_TAB1)
  public readonly i18nTab1$: Observable<I18nTab1>,
) {}

And finally, wrap component.html with ng-container and a simple ngIf statement:

<ng-container *ngIf="{
    core: i18nCore$ | async,
    tab1: i18nTab1$ | async
  } as i18n">
    <p>{{ i18n.core?.language }}</p>
    <p>{{ i18n.tab1?.country }}: n/a</p>
</ng-container>

Checking out the result

Let’s run this and see if this actually works and more importantly how exactly would these translations be loaded. I created a simple demo app consisting of two lazy-loaded Angular modules, so you may clone and experiment with it. But for now, here are the actual screenshots of DevTools:

This is the initial page load in development mode; note the two .js files at the very end — we created these in a previous section.

This is what it looks like when language is being switched. The Network tab has been reset for demonstration purposes.

And this is the result of switching to the second lazy tab.

Benefits

  • With this solution you would be able but not obliged to split your translations into several files in any way that you need;
  • It’s reactive, which means that being implemented correctly it provides your users with a seamless experience;
  • It does not require you to install anything that doesn’t ship with Angular out of the box;
  • It’s easily debuggable and fully customizable as it would be implemented directly in your project;
  • It supports complex locale resolutions like relating on browser language, picking up regional settings from user account upon authorization and overriding with a user-defined language — and all this without a single page reload;
  • It also supports code completion in modern IDEs.

Drawbacks

  • As these translation files will not be included in assets, they should actually be transpiled which will slightly increase the build time;
  • It requires you to create a custom utility or use a third-party solution to exchange your translations with a localization platform;
  • It might not work really well with search engines without proper server-side rendering.

GitHub

Feel free to experiment with the fully working example that is available in this repository.
Stay positive and create great apps!

Cover photo by Nareeta Martin on Unsplash

Bogdan Alexandru Militaru - Jul 13

Thiago Silva Lopes - Aug 4

shrey vijayvargiya - Aug 5

item.title: JS中2个相同值的数组不相等。 | 2 arrays of Same value are not equal in JS.
item.description:
神秘客

发表于大家好，今天我将讨论一些你可能在 Javascript 中没有注意到的小事。
我会用一个例子向你展示那件事。

示例 1 -

 let array1 = [ 1 , 5 , 9 , 14 , 17 ]; let array2 = [ 1 , 5 , 9 , 14 , 17 ]; console . log ( array1 == array2 ); console . log ( array1 === array2 );

你能猜到输出会是什么吗？

输出 -

  • 虽然值相等，但它在两个比较中都返回 false，元素的数量也相等，为什么它返回 false？ 因为 javascript 中的所有内容都是对象，而数组也是对象，因此它不是比较元素的值或数量，而是检查那些不同的数组的引用，这就是为什么它在两种情况下都返回 false 的原因。

示例 2 -

 let array1 = [ 1 , 5 , 9 , 14 , 17 ]; let array2 = [ 1 , 5 , 9 , 14 , 17 ]; let array3 = array1 console . log ( array3 === array1 ); console . log ( array3 === array2 );

你现在能猜到输出会是什么吗？

输出 -

  • 那么它在比较array3和array1时返回true的原因是因为我们将array1的引用存储在array3中，所以两者都指向同一个引用，而array3和array2比较返回false，因为它们有不同的引用。

那么如何比较这些数组呢？

答案很简单，使用toString方法将数组转换成字符串，然后进行比较

示例 3-

 let array1 = [ 1 , 5 , 9 , 14 , 17 ]; let array2 = [ 1 , 5 , 9 , 14 , 17 ]; console . log ( array1 . toString () === array2 . toString ());

输出 -

  • 所以，现在它正在比较相等的字符串值，这就是它这次返回 true 的原因。

它看起来很混乱，我尽力解释，如果您发现任何错误，请在评论部分更正。

感谢您查看这篇文章^^您可以通过以下链接捐款帮助我谢谢👇👇 ^^

还要检查这些帖子
一旦暂停，shubhamtiwari909 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，shubhamtiwari909 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，shubhamtiwari909 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 shubhamtiwari909 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 shubhamtiwari909 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 shubhamtiwari909 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Mysterio

Posted on Aug 12

Hello Guys today i am going to discuss a very little thing that you might have not noticed in Javascript.
I will show you that thing using an example.

Example 1 - 

let array1= [1,5,9,14,17];
let array2= [1,5,9,14,17]; console.log(array1 == array2);
console.log(array1 === array2);

Can you guess what will be the output?

Output -

  • It returned false in both comparsion although the values are equals, number of elements are also equal so, why it returned false? because everything in javascript is an object and arrays are also objects therefore instead of comparing the values or number of elements , it checks the reference of those array which is different that's why it returned false in both the cases.

Example 2 - 

let array1= [1,5,9,14,17];
let array2= [1,5,9,14,17];
let array3 = array1
console.log(array3 === array1);
console.log(array3 === array2);

Can you guess now what will be the output?

Output -

  • Well the reason it returned true while comparing array3 with array1 is because we stored the reference of array1 in array3 so, both are pointing to the same reference while array3 and array2 comparison returned false because they have different references.

How you can then compare these arrays?

The answer is simple , use the toString method to convert the array into strings and then compare them

Example 3- 

let array1= [1,5,9,14,17];
let array2= [1,5,9,14,17];
console.log(array1.toString() === array2.toString());

Output -

  • So, now it is comparing string values which is equal that's why it returned true this time.

It looks quite confusing and i tried my best to explain and if you find any point wrong please correct it in the comment section.

THANK YOU FOR CHECKING THIS POST ^^You can help me by some donation at the link below Thank you👇👇 ^^

☕ --> https://www.buymeacoffee.com/waaduheck <--

Also check these posts as well
https://dev.to/shubhamtiwari909/javascript-map-with-filter-2jgo

item.title: 阿布赛义德（网站欺骗）当心 | Abu Sayed (Website Spoofing) Beware
item.description:
您好，我正在与您可能以前听说过的一个人打交道。 这家伙正在从 dev.to 窃取文章并为了自己的利益而更改它们。 如果有人有他的联系方式，我想得到它，这样我就可以警告他，我正在考虑将来起诉他。 我似乎找不到它，他创建的网站已被感染并且有弹出窗口。

我的文章/网站： https ://dev.to/devknight/how-i-became-a-freelance-web-developer-and-designer-on-upwork-with-no-experience-you-can-too-3ife ?

一旦暂停，devknight 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devknight 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devknight 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devknight 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 devknight 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 devknight 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hello, I'm dealing with an individual that you have probably heard of before. This guy is stealing articles from dev.to and changing them for his own benefit. If anyone has his contact information, I'd like to obtain it so I can warn him that I'm considering prosecuting against him in the future. I can't seem to find it, the websites he creates are infected and have popups.

(Make sure you have a strong firewall and antivirus; be careful the site isn't safe) Abu Sayed site: https://sayed.work/dev-feed/how-i-grew-to-become-a-freelance-web-developer-and-designer-on-upwork-with-no-expertise-you-can-too/

My article/website: https://dev.to/devknight/how-i-became-a-freelance-web-developer-and-designer-on-upwork-with-no-experience-you-can-too-3ife?

Once suspended, devknight will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devknight will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devknight will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devknight is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag devknight:

devknight consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging devknight will restore default visibility to their posts.
